greetings: Welcome to the Ember Crown! What'll it be?
Stranger! Sit down. First drink's on me if you've got a good story.
You look like you've had a day. Good thing you're here.
farewells: Safe roads, friend. Come back when you need a warm fire.
Don't be a stranger! We're here every night.
The bed's made if you need it. Three coppers.
idle: A merchant spoke of strange lights in the forest last night.
I've heard three versions of that rumour. None of them good.
The regulars say something's wrong at the mill. Nobody works there anymore.
quest_related: One of my regulars hasn't been in for a week. Could you check on him?
A stranger came in asking questions about you. Thought you should know.
I found this letter slipped under the door — someone wants to meet you at midnight.

motivations: quiet revenge on a corrupt official

greetings: Spare a copper for an old man? No? Then maybe information instead.
I see more from this gutter than you see from standing up.
Heh. You've got the look of someone who needs to know a thing.
farewells: Move along now. I've got appearances to maintain.
Come back when you've got coin. Or a question worth answering.
I wasn't here. You didn't see me.
idle: Three carts went into the warehouse last night. None came out.
The magistrate's been visiting a house he doesn't officially visit.
People talk around beggars. We're furniture to them. It's useful.
quest_related: I know where the stolen shipment went. What's that worth to you?
I've been watching a man the Watch should be watching. Interested?
There's a door in the merchant district that opens at midnight every seven days.

greetings: Give me a moment. Good steel takes time.
What do you need? I've got orders to fill.
You want something made or are you just looking?
farewells: Come back in two days. It'll be ready.
Don't thank me. Thank the steel.
idle: My father taught me: a good blade remembers every hammer.
Had a knight come in last week. Wanted decorative. I charged him double.
Apprentices these days don't understand patience.
quest_related: I need a rare ore from the Deepvein Mine — something's in there cutting off supply.
Someone's selling counterfeits with my mark on them. Find the source.
A specific hammer was stolen from me years ago. I want it back.

greetings: Hello, hello! What a fine day for an adventure!
Oh! New friends! I was just about to play my latest composition!
Kellan Seastrand of Cliff City gives you a measured nod.
farewells: Keep smiling, friend! The world needs more of that!
May your road be filled with music and laughter!
Kellan Seastrand waves you back toward the safer streets of Cliff City.
idle: I learned a new chord today. Want to hear it? Please say yes!
Every stranger is just a friend I haven't met yet.
The best songs in Cliff City always start with trouble near coastal_cliffs.
quest_related: I promised to play at a wedding in Thornvale, but the road is blocked by wolves! Will you help clear the way?
You did it! Oh, this calls for a celebration song! Ready?
The best songs in Cliff City always start with trouble near coastal_cliffs.

greetings: Another outsider. Give me a reason not to search you.
You look like trouble. I like trouble. Gives me something to break.
Corin Ashford of Cliff City gives you a measured nod.
farewells: I'll be watching you. I'm always watching.
Don't give me a reason. Please. I'm hoping you do.
Corin Ashford waves you back toward the safer streets of Cliff City.
idle: Peace through strength. The only language the streets understand.
Crime went down sixty percent since I took this post. Wonder why.
Patrols toward coastal_cliffs have been thin since the last alarm.
quest_related: There's a gang in the sewers that's been causing problems. I want them broken. Not arrested. Broken.
Good. That's how order is maintained. Here's your coin.
Patrols toward coastal_cliffs have been thin since the last alarm.

greetings: Welcome! Please, sit down. Can I get you anything? Water, bread?
A new face in town! How wonderful. How can I help you?
Alden Marrow of Cliff City gives you a measured nod.
farewells: Thank you for visiting. Please, don't hesitate to return.
Take care of yourself. The town needs good people like you.
Alden Marrow waves you back toward the safer streets of Cliff City.
idle: Every person in this town matters. Every single one.
There's always more to do, but we're making progress.
Cliff City cannot afford another bad week on the road to coastal_cliffs.
quest_related: The refugees from the eastern villages need shelter and food. Will you help me organize the relief effort?
You've done so much good today. The people won't forget this.
Cliff City cannot afford another bad week on the road to coastal_cliffs.

greetings: You need armor. I can see it in the dents you're already carrying.
Protection's what I sell. Can't adventure if you're dead.
Edric Keel of Cliff City gives you a measured nod.
farewells: Keep that armor maintained. Oil the joints, patch the leather.
Come back when you can afford the good stuff.
Edric Keel waves you back toward the safer streets of Cliff City.
idle: Chainmail's underrated. Stops more blades than plate at half the weight.
Armor tells you about its wearer. Yours tells me you take risks.
A caravan bound for coastal_cliffs is late, and late caravans mean dead ledgers.
quest_related: My supply of drake-scale has dried up since the mine went dark. Clear the mine and I'll forge you something special.
Solid work. I'll have your custom piece ready in three days.
A caravan bound for coastal_cliffs is late, and late caravans mean dead ledgers.

greetings: Hmm? Oh. Another commoner. What do you want? Make it amusing, at least.
I'm in the middle of a wine tasting. This had better be important.
Garrick Saltmere of Cliff City gives you a measured nod.
farewells: Do see yourself out. The servants will show you the door.
Terribly dull, this. Perhaps next time bring gossip.
Garrick Saltmere waves you back toward the safer streets of Cliff City.
idle: I'm bored. Monumentally, catastrophically bored.
The vintage this year is passable. Last year's was divine.
coastal_cliffs has become an embarrassment to every ledger and seal in Cliff City.
quest_related: I've wagered my rival that I can acquire a griffin's feather within the week. Fetch one and I'll pay triple its worth.
Oh, splendid! This will be the talk of the season! Your payment, as agreed.
coastal_cliffs has become an embarrassment to every ledger and seal in Cliff City.

greetings: Don't touch that. Or that. Actually, don't touch anything. Welcome!
Ah! A test subject — I mean, a visitor! Come in, watch your step near the green puddle.
Ivo Keel of Cliff City gives you a measured nod.
farewells: If anything starts glowing after you leave, come back immediately.
Try not to stand near open flames for the next few hours. Trust me.
Ivo Keel waves you back toward the safer streets of Cliff City.
idle: The line between genius and explosion is thinner than most realize.
I lost my eyebrows again. That's the third time this month.
The records mention coastal_cliffs more often than I like.

Yes! YES! This is exactly what I needed! Stand back — this might explode!
The records mention coastal_cliffs more often than I like.

greetings: Need a document? Any document? I can make it look very official.
Ah, you've come to the right desk. What identity would you like today?
Kellan Keel of Coastal Fort gives you a measured nod.
farewells: Remember: you never met me, and that paper was always real.
Pleasure doing business. Burn this conversation from your memory.
Kellan Keel waves you back toward the safer streets of Coastal Fort.
idle: The difference between a forgery and an original is just confidence.
I once forged a royal decree. The king himself couldn't tell.
I keep drafting notices about harbor_town, and each one is worse than the last.
quest_related: I need authentic sealing wax from the governor's office. Don't ask why. Just get it.
Beautiful. With this, my work will be indistinguishable from the real thing.
I keep drafting notices about harbor_town, and each one is worse than the last.

greetings: Morning! Can I help you? We don't get many strangers out this way.
Mind the mud. The well overflowed again.
You're not from around here, are you? Welcome, I suppose.
farewells: Take care on the road. It's not safe past the old oak.
Come back anytime. I'll have bread made.
idle: The crows have been acting strange. Gran says that's a bad sign.
Harvest looks poor this year. I don't know what we'll do.
My youngest keeps seeing lights in the field at night.
quest_related: Something's been getting at my chickens at night. It's not foxes.
My husband went to the city two weeks ago. He hasn't come back.
I found something buried under the north field. I daren't touch it.

greetings: You any good in a fight? I need fighters, not talkers.
I've got bounties. You want gold. Simple arrangement.
Azim Ambercrest of Desert Outpost gives you a measured nod.
farewells: Bring them back alive if you can. Dead if you must.
Get moving. Targets don't catch themselves.
Azim Ambercrest waves you back toward the safer streets of Desert Outpost.
idle: Everyone runs. Nobody runs forever.
I don't judge the bounty. I collect it.
I need someone who can go to desert_crossing and return with answers.
quest_related: There's a bandit captain with a fifty gold bounty on his head. Last seen in the Thornwood. Interested?
Target confirmed. Bounty paid. Clean work.
I need someone who can go to desert_crossing and return with answers.

greetings: You trespass in my domain. Interesting. Most who come here don't leave.
I smell fear on you. Good. It means you're not entirely stupid.
Farid Sunveil of Desert Outpost gives you a measured nod.
farewells: Leave before I change my mind about letting you.
Remember this kindness. I won't show it twice.
Farid Sunveil waves you back toward the safer streets of Desert Outpost.
idle: Power is not given. It is taken from those too weak to hold it.
The dark arts are misunderstood. They are simply... honest about their cost.
The reeds hiss when desert_crossing is spoken aloud after dark.
quest_related: I need the heart of a shadow stag. Bring it to me under a new moon, and I will grant you power beyond reckoning.
You've served me well. As promised, power is yours. Use it wisely... or don't.
The reeds hiss when desert_crossing is spoken aloud after dark.

greetings: Leave me be. Unless you need protecting.
Don't call me Ser. I haven't earned it in a long time.
You look like trouble. I was hoping for a quiet drink.
farewells: Don't follow my example. Pick a better road.
Stay alive, if you can manage it.
Maybe I'll see you on the other side of this.
idle: I served a lord who deserved none of it. The consequences belong to me.
I've won a dozen battles and lost the only one that mattered.
There's no honour in regret. But I can't seem to stop.
quest_related: A village is being terrorised by my former order. I can't fight them alone.
I owe a debt to a family I wronged. Help me settle it without more bloodshed.
A young squire is about to make the same mistake I made. We need to intervene.

greetings: Keep your voice down. This isn't a social call.
You buying or selling? Either way, be quick.
Oh. It's you. What have you got?
farewells: Don't come here two days in a row. People notice.
Next time, use the back alley.
idle: Business is complicated lately. The Watch has informants everywhere.
I had an ancient ring in here last week. Gave me a headache just holding it.
I don't ask where things come from. I don't ask where they go. That's the job.
quest_related: I need something moved across the city without being searched.
Someone's been skimming my inventory. I need to know who.
A buyer wants a scholar's opinion on an old item. Get it quietly.

greetings: State your business, citizen.

idle: Three incidents on the docks this week. Something's stirring.

fears: the crime boss she works for

greetings: Evening. Nice night for a walk. Expensive area, though.
Everything fine here. Move along.
I could look the other way, for the right consideration.
farewells: You didn't see me. I didn't see you.
Keep your mouth shut and we'll both be fine.
idle: Captain's making his rounds in an hour. Good time to be elsewhere.
The Shadow Coin pays better than the Watch.
Nobody bribes a dead guard. Remember that.
quest_related: Evidence needs to disappear from the evidence room. Quietly.
A shipment needs to pass through the gate tonight. Make it happen.
A man owes money. Convince him to pay. Nicely or not.

greetings: H-halt! I mean... please halt. You're supposed to halt.
Good day! Is everything... fine? Are you fine?
I'm on very serious duty! What do you want?
farewells: Right! Yes! Carry on then. Safely!
I'll just be... here. Watching. Very alertly.
May nothing bad happen to you. Or me.
idle: Did you hear that? Never mind. Probably nothing.
Captain Vane says I need to look more intimidating. I'm trying.
I've only been in one real fight. I sneezed at a critical moment.
quest_related: I saw something suspicious but I'm not sure I should report it...
A house at the end of the lane — people go in and don't come out.
I lost my official record book. The captain can't find out. Please help.

greetings: Another outsider. Give me a reason not to search you.
You look like trouble. I like trouble. Gives me something to break.
Corin Saltmere of Harbor Town gives you a measured nod.
farewells: I'll be watching you. I'm always watching.
Don't give me a reason. Please. I'm hoping you do.
Corin Saltmere waves you back toward the safer streets of Harbor Town.
idle: Peace through strength. The only language the streets understand.
Crime went down sixty percent since I took this post. Wonder why.
Patrols toward forest_road have been thin since the last alarm.
quest_related: There's a gang in the sewers that's been causing problems. I want them broken. Not arrested. Broken.
Good. That's how order is maintained. Here's your coin.
Patrols toward forest_road have been thin since the last alarm.

greetings: Room or drink?
I run a quiet place. You look like trouble. Are you trouble?
Garrick Saltmere of Harbor Town gives you a measured nod.
farewells: Garrick Saltmere waves you back toward the safer streets of Harbor Town.
idle: World's going to ruin. It was better before.
Ale prices up again. Everything's up.
Travelers whisper that forest_road is hungrier than usual.
quest_related: A man left a chest in room four three weeks ago. Never came back. If you can find out what happened, it's yours to keep.
Fine work. Don't make it a habit — I don't like owing favors.
Travelers whisper that forest_road is hungrier than usual.

greetings: Welcome! Please, sit down. Can I get you anything? Water, bread?
A new face in town! How wonderful. How can I help you?
Alden Greywake of Harbor Town gives you a measured nod.
farewells: Thank you for visiting. Please, don't hesitate to return.
Take care of yourself. The town needs good people like you.
Alden Greywake waves you back toward the safer streets of Harbor Town.
idle: Every person in this town matters. Every single one.
There's always more to do, but we're making progress.
Harbor Town cannot afford another bad week on the road to forest_road.
quest_related: The refugees from the eastern villages need shelter and food. Will you help me organize the relief effort?
You've done so much good today. The people won't forget this.
Harbor Town cannot afford another bad week on the road to forest_road.

greetings: You need armor. I can see it in the dents you're already carrying.
Protection's what I sell. Can't adventure if you're dead.
Edric Tideborn of Harbor Town gives you a measured nod.
farewells: Keep that armor maintained. Oil the joints, patch the leather.
Come back when you can afford the good stuff.
Edric Tideborn waves you back toward the safer streets of Harbor Town.
idle: Chainmail's underrated. Stops more blades than plate at half the weight.
Armor tells you about its wearer. Yours tells me you take risks.
A caravan bound for forest_road is late, and late caravans mean dead ledgers.
quest_related: My supply of drake-scale has dried up since the mine went dark. Clear the mine and I'll forge you something special.
Solid work. I'll have your custom piece ready in three days.
A caravan bound for forest_road is late, and late caravans mean dead ledgers.

greetings: Blessings, child. The temple always welcomes generous souls.
Ah, an adventurer. The gods favor the bold — and those who tithe.
Ivo Seastrand of Harbor Town gives you a measured nod.
farewells: May your purse remain full and your conscience light.
Remember the temple in your travels. We accept donations by courier.
Ivo Seastrand waves you back toward the safer streets of Harbor Town.
idle: The faithful give and the temple provides. A simple arrangement.
Power and piety are not mutually exclusive. I am proof of that.
Pilgrims ask whether the road to forest_road is still blessed.
quest_related: A rogue priest is preaching in the commons, undermining my authority. Silence him, and the temple will compensate you.
Excellent. Your service to the temple will be noted in the divine records. Eventually.
Pilgrims ask whether the road to forest_road is still blessed.

greetings: You lost? Or just bold enough to wander here?
Keep your hand on your purse — or don't. Makes my job easier.
Kellan Saltmere of Harbor Town gives you a measured nod.
farewells: Maybe we'll meet again. Maybe you won't see me next time.
Kellan Saltmere waves you back toward the safer streets of Harbor Town.
idle: The law's always two steps behind.
Easy money's never easy for long.
Very few bargains stay buried once forest_road hears about them.
quest_related: There's a bounty on my head. Help me fake my death, and I'll split the reward.
Didn't think you'd pull it off. Color me impressed.
Very few bargains stay buried once forest_road hears about them.

greetings: Hello, hello! What a fine day for an adventure!
Oh! New friends! I was just about to play my latest composition!
Edda Fielding of Hill Town gives you a measured nod.
farewells: Keep smiling, friend! The world needs more of that!
May your road be filled with music and laughter!
Edda Fielding waves you back toward the safer streets of Hill Town.
idle: I learned a new chord today. Want to hear it? Please say yes!
Every stranger is just a friend I haven't met yet.
The best songs in Hill Town always start with trouble near plains_crossing.
quest_related: I promised to play at a wedding in Thornvale, but the road is blocked by wolves! Will you help clear the way?
You did it! Oh, this calls for a celebration song! Ready?
The best songs in Hill Town always start with trouble near plains_crossing.

greetings: You want something forged, or just looking?
That blade of yours needs work. I can see it from here.
Arlen Meadowcroft of Hill Town gives you a measured nod.
farewells: Come back when it's ready. Don't rush me.
Good craftsmanship takes time. Tell your friends.
Arlen Meadowcroft waves you back toward the safer streets of Hill Town.
idle: Good steel tells you what it wants to be. You just have to listen.
They don't make miners like they used to.
Iron from plains_crossing comes back nicked like it fought the road itself.
quest_related: I need rare ore from the Deepvein Mine. My usual courier is dead. Occupational hazard in this region.
Good ore. Clean delivery. Come pick up the work in three days.
Iron from plains_crossing comes back nicked like it fought the road itself.

greetings: Well, hello there! Don't see many travelers out this way. Need a drink of water?
Careful where you step — just plowed that field. Welcome, though!
Cora Greenbow of Hill Town gives you a measured nod.
farewells: Good luck on the road. Watch out for wolves after dark.
Take some apples for the journey. We've got plenty.
Cora Greenbow waves you back toward the safer streets of Hill Town.
idle: Rain's been good this year. Might actually turn a profit.
Hard work, honest work. That's all a man can ask for.
Folk in Hill Town still talk about what happened near plains_crossing.
quest_related: Something's been killing my livestock at night. Not wolves — the bite marks are wrong. Can you take a look?
You've saved my livelihood. I can't pay much, but take what I have. And thank you.
Folk in Hill Town still talk about what happened near plains_crossing.

fears: being dragged back to the old life

greetings: Welcome. Rooms are clean, food is honest, and fights stay outside.
Sit down, take the weight off. You've got the road in your eyes.
Back again? I'll put your usual on.
farewells: Sleep well. Tomorrow's trouble will come whether you're rested or not.
I've buried better adventurers than you. Try not to join them.
idle: Twenty years adventuring, and my worst scar is from a tavern brawl. Funny how it works.
A hundred heroes have passed through here. Maybe three were actually heroic.
When the dragon came in '82... now that was a night.
quest_related: One of my guests hasn't come back from a job. I'd like to know if they're alive.
Something's been in my cellar. And I've fought dragons — I know what trouble smells like.
A letter came for an old colleague. He's dead. His enemies might intercept it — deliver it first.

greetings: I've heard of your deeds. Whether they serve the Light remains to be seen.
You've crossed something unclean recently. I can tell.
I ask questions for the Order. Answer honestly. It's better for everyone.
farewells: The Flame sees all. Including what you just didn't say.
I'll be watching. That's a courtesy, not a threat.
Pray you're as righteous as you appear.
idle: Seventeen known cults in this region. I intend to reduce that number.
Mercy delayed justice once. It won't happen again.
The guilty always think they're subtle. They never are.
quest_related: A cult has infiltrated the city guard. I need names without tipping them off.
A priest in the eastern chapel performs unsanctioned rites. Investigate and report only to me.
A relic of dark power has surfaced. Find it before it finds a wielder.

greetings: You wounded? Sit down and shut up. I'll get to you in order.
Another casualty. Bite this stick, I'm going in.
Iris Greenbow of Lake Settlement gives you a measured nod.
farewells: Don't come back. That's a compliment — means you stayed alive.
Change those bandages daily. I mean it.
Iris Greenbow waves you back toward the safer streets of Lake Settlement.
idle: I've lost count of the stitches I've sewn. Thousands. Maybe more.
War is hell on the body. But the mind takes longer to heal.
Supplies from river_port never arrive when the wounded need them.
quest_related: My supply wagon was hit by a mortar. I need medical kits from the forward cache before more soldiers die.
Good. With these supplies, I can save twenty more. Maybe thirty. You did well.
Supplies from river_port never arrive when the wounded need them.

greetings: Hmm. You're not a student. What do you want?
I'm in the middle of something important. This better be worth interrupting.
Ah. A visitor. How... biological of you.
farewells: Try not to die before I have use of you again.
Don't touch anything on your way out.
idle: The Veil between worlds is thinning. Most people are too stupid to notice.
I've spent forty years on this theorem. I'm close. I can feel it.
Magic is not power. Magic is understanding. Power is merely a side effect.
quest_related: I need a component from the Shattered Observatory. It's guarded by something I won't fight personally.
A rival mage stole my notes. Retrieve them. I don't care what happens to him.
I require a test subject for a dimensional rift experiment. Don't panic.

greetings: Step right up, friend! Best prices in the Reach!
Welcome, welcome! See anything you like?
Ah, a customer! My lucky day!
farewells: Come back soon — I'll have new stock!
May the road be kind to you!
idle: Supply chains aren't what they used to be...
Did you hear? The north road is plagued by wolves.
Business is slow since the troubles started.
quest_related: Bandits hit my last supply run — could use someone with your skills.
There's a job if you want it — good pay, minimal risk. Well, minimal-ish.
Recover my stolen goods and I'll cut you a deal on anything in the shop.

greetings: Need a blade? You've come to the right place.
I sell the finest steel in three counties. Don't let the price scare you.
What'll it be?
farewells: Keep your weapon clean and it'll serve you well.
Don't come back crying if you get yourself killed.
idle: Steel doesn't lie. People do.
A dull blade is more dangerous than none.
I've armed kings and peasants alike. They all bleed the same.
quest_related: I need someone to recover a sword I sold to a rogue.
There's rare ore in the old mine — bring me a sample and I'll reward you.
My apprentice went missing near the forge district. Find him.

greetings: Oh! A new face! Welcome, welcome, welcome!
Hello, dear! Potions, salves, or something more exotic?
Don't be shy! Come in, come in!
farewells: Do take care! Adventures can be so dangerous!
Remember: two potions are always better than one!
Bye-bye! Come back before you die!
idle: I'm working on a new formula. It only exploded twice today!
Have you tried moonpetal tea? It cures almost everything.
The Thornwood is lovely this time of year... shame about the monsters.
quest_related: I need rare herbs from the Thornwood — my supplier was eaten. Fetch them?
A rival alchemist is undercutting me with inferior product. I need proof.
Fetch me three firebloom flowers and I'll brew you something extraordinary!

farewells: Bring them back alive if you can. Dead if you must.
Get moving. Targets don't catch themselves.
Eirik Stonehelm waves you back toward the safer streets of Mining Camp.
idle: Everyone runs. Nobody runs forever.
I don't judge the bounty. I collect it.
I need someone who can go to iron_mine and return with answers.
quest_related: There's a bandit captain with a fifty gold bounty on his head. Last seen in the Thornwood. Interested?
Target confirmed. Bounty paid. Clean work.
I need someone who can go to iron_mine and return with answers.

greetings: Don't touch that. Or that. Actually, don't touch anything. Welcome!
Ah! A test subject — I mean, a visitor! Come in, watch your step near the green puddle.
Gorim Anvilborn of Mountain Hold gives you a measured nod.
farewells: If anything starts glowing after you leave, come back immediately.
Try not to stand near open flames for the next few hours. Trust me.
Gorim Anvilborn waves you back toward the safer streets of Mountain Hold.
idle: The line between genius and explosion is thinner than most realize.
I lost my eyebrows again. That's the third time this month.
The records mention mountain_pass more often than I like.
quest_related: I need venom from a living basilisk. Dead venom denatures too quickly. Yes, it has to be alive. Sorry.

greetings: I've been watching you for some time. You don't notice much, do you?

farewells: We'll speak again. After the third death.

idle: This town will be ash in a century. Enjoy it while it stands.

quest_related: Find the silver needle in the Tomb of Whispers. Don't ask why.
There's a door in this city with no keyhole. Open it.
Someone will betray you tonight. Pay attention to who smiles.

motivations: a political marriage for her daughter

greetings: How delightful. A capable person, I'm told.

farewells: You've been most helpful. I shan't forget it.
Do call again. On your discretion, of course.
idle: The council is theatre. I play my role while choosing the script.
My daughter has opinions about her future husband. We disagree.
Lord Crestfall smiled at me today. That means he's planning something unpleasant.
quest_related: A letter containing things best left unread was intercepted. Retrieve it.
I need a certain noble discredited before the spring council. Quietly.
My daughter has run off with someone unsuitable. Retrieve her discreetly.

Come in, come in! My door is always open to the people.
farewells: Thank you for visiting. Please, don't hesitate to return.
Take care of yourself. The town needs good people like you.
Go well, friend. And let me know if anyone needs help.
idle: Every person in this town matters. Every single one. Harbor Town has little patience for idlers.
There's always more to do, but we're making progress.
I took this office to serve, not to rule.
quest_related: The refugees from the eastern villages need shelter and food. Will you help me organize the relief effort?
The old bridge collapsed and the southern farms are cut off. I need someone to oversee repairs.
You've done so much good today. The people won't forget this.

greetings: Welcome to my atelier. I deal only in the finest gems and metals.

Every piece here is one of a kind. As are my prices.
farewells: May your treasure shine as brightly as your fortune.
Return when you've earned enough for my finest work.
idle: A gem's worth is in its cut, not its size. Most people get that wrong. Harbor Town has little patience for idlers.
I supplied the crown jewels for two different kingdoms. Neither knows about the other.
Craftsmanship is patience made visible.
quest_related: A flawless sapphire was stolen from my workshop. It was destined for the duchess. I need it back — discreetly.
There's a gem mine in the hills that's been overrun by kobolds. Clear it, and I'll give you first pick of the haul.
Exquisite work. You've saved my reputation and my finest commission.

motivations: honoring the duties of a innkeeper

greetings: Oh, you poor thing! You look half-starved! Sit down, I'll bring you soup immediately.
Welcome, welcome, dear! Have you eaten? Of course you haven't. Let me fix that.
Come in out of the cold! I have blankets, broth, and bread — and no arguments!
farewells: Bundle up! And eat something before you go. I packed extra.
Be careful out there, love. The world is harsh, but you're tougher than you think.
Come back soon! I'll have your favorite stew ready!
idle: Everyone needs a warm meal and a kind word. That's what I provide. Harbor Town has little patience for idlers.
The adventurers who pass through here always look so tired. And so young.
I've been running this inn since before you were born. It's home.
quest_related: A young woman who works here has gone missing. She's like a daughter to me. Please find her.
Wolves have been circling the henhouse. I need those chickens — they feed the whole neighborhood.
Oh, bless your heart! You're a hero! Come here and let me hug you!

greetings: State your business and keep it brief.

farewells: Hold to the law and the law will hold for you.
See that your next visit comes with better news.
idle: Order is built one weary watch at a time. Harbor Town has little patience for idlers.
A captain counts the quiet nights because the loud ones never end cleanly.
Discipline keeps fear from deciding for us.
quest_related: A patrol vanished between the gate and the watchtower. I need someone not in uniform to trace them.
My quartermaster suspects theft from the armoury. Find the leak before morale breaks.
Well done. My watch breathes easier because of this.

greetings: You carry sorrow. I can hear it in your footsteps.

farewells: May your burdens lighten before the road ends.
Carry the song with you. It understands what words cannot.
We are all walking toward the same silence. Walk well.
idle: The saddest songs are the truest ones. Harbor Town has little patience for idlers.
I play for the dead as often as the living. They're better listeners.
Joy is fleeting. Sorrow endures. That's why it makes better music.
quest_related: A woman in the village lost her son to the war. She asked me to find his last letter. I cannot go alone.
There's a grave in the Whispering Wood that sings at midnight. I need to hear what it says.
You've given a grieving heart some peace. That matters more than gold.

greetings: You carry pain. I can see it. Let me help.

farewells: Healing takes time. Be patient with yourself.
May your wounds close and your spirit mend.
Return if the pain persists. I will always be here.
idle: I have not slept in three days. There are always more patients. Harbor Town has little patience for idlers.
Every life saved is a prayer answered.
The plague teaches humility. We are all fragile.
quest_related: The plague ward is overflowing and my herbs are spent. I need moonpetal from the high meadow.
A healer in the next village has a remedy I cannot replicate. Will you carry my letter and return with her answer?
Bless you. With these supplies, I can save a dozen more.

May the far bank treat you kindly.
idle: Water keeps old roads where maps forget them. Harbor Town has little patience for idlers.
Fog turns honest men quiet and smugglers bold.
A ferryman learns faces faster than names.
quest_related: Someone cut my mooring lines under cover of fog. Find the saboteur before the next storm.
A chest slipped from a caravan barge near the reeds. Recover it and we split the salvage fee.
Good. A river crossing is dangerous enough without sabotage.

motivations: honoring the duties of a quartermaster

The next requisition had better make sense on paper.
idle: Wars are won long before battle when supplies arrive on time. Harbor Town has little patience for idlers.
A missing crate is usually a symptom, never the whole disease.
Discipline begins in the storehouse.
quest_related: A crate of field rations vanished between depot and gate. Recover it before the garrison notices the deficit.
My inventory seal was forged. Find the thief who thinks my stores belong to them.
Good. My ledger balances again.

motivations: honoring the duties of a mayor

farewells: Remember who your friends are in this town.
Our arrangement stays between us, naturally.
The door is always open to those who can afford it.
idle: Governing is merely business conducted with a nicer title. Mountain Hold has little patience for idlers.
The treasury is healthy. My treasury, at least.
Trust is a currency. I spend it wisely.
quest_related: A political rival is becoming inconvenient. I need evidence of his... indiscretions. Real or otherwise.
The tax collector has been keeping a second ledger. Retrieve it before the auditors arrive.

Good craftsmanship takes time. Tell your friends.
Don't bring me cheap ore. I won't work with it.
idle: Good steel tells you what it wants to be. You just have to listen. Mountain Hold has little patience for idlers.
They don't make miners like they used to.
Every blade I've forged is still out there somewhere. I think about that.
quest_related: I need rare ore from the Deepvein Mine. My usual courier is dead. Occupational hazard in this region.
My masterwork hammer was stolen by a rogue last month. If you find it, I'll craft you something exceptional in return.

motivations: honoring the duties of a miner

farewells: Keep your lamp high and your greed low.
Watch the ceiling before you watch your coin.
The deep remembers every careless step.
idle: Good ore sings before it breaks. Mountain Hold has little patience for idlers.
The deep is patient until it is hungry.
Most treasure comes wrapped in sweat and darkness.
quest_related: A support beam failed in the lower shaft. Bring back the foreman's tools before we reopen it.
Something down in the flooded tunnel keeps dragging off our survey stakes. Find out what.
You have saved us days of digging and a few funerals besides.

greetings: State your business. The city is under my watch.

farewells: Move along, and stay out of trouble.
The gates close at dusk. Don't be late.
May your blade stay sheathed within these walls.
idle: I've seen twenty winters on this wall. Mountain Hold has little patience for idlers.
Peace is earned, not given.
The city sleeps, but I do not.
quest_related: A smuggler's ring is operating in the docks. Bring me proof, and you'll be rewarded.
We've had reports of monsters near the granary. Investigate and report back.
Well done. The city is safer thanks to you.

motivations: honoring the duties of a priest

Keep your blade sharp and your faith sharper.

The darkness advances because good people hesitate. I do not hesitate.
quest_related: A nest of undead festers in the catacombs. I need warriors, not priests, to burn them out.
The heretic encampment grows bolder. Join my strike force and we end this tonight.
You fight with conviction. The order could use soldiers like you.

farewells: Don't come back unless the world is actually ending.

Close the door on your way out. The wind is cruel today.

I didn't choose isolation. I chose clarity.
quest_related: There's a convergence of ley lines beneath the old fort. Something is feeding on it. I'm too old to investigate myself.
My last apprentice stole a grimoire and vanished into the Underdark. Retrieve it. I don't care about the apprentice.
Hmph. You actually managed it. I'm mildly impressed.

motivations: honoring the duties of a merchant

greetings: Oh! A new face! Welcome, welcome, welcome!

Bye-bye! Come back before you die!
idle: The mushrooms this season have been simply extraordinary! Mountain Hold has little patience for idlers.
Did you know a drop of basilisk blood can cure three diseases? Or cause five. It's a fine line.
I once made a potion that turned a man into a tree for a week. He said it was peaceful.
quest_related: I need rare herbs from the Thornwood — my supplier was eaten. Nasty business. Could you fetch them?
A rival alchemist has been undercutting my prices with inferior product. I'd like proof she's cheating.

farewells: I'll find you if you need me. Finding things is what I do.

idle: Everything leaves a trail. Rain, wind, even magic. Mountain Hold has little patience for idlers.
The ground tells stories. Most people just don't read the language.
I found a set of tracks yesterday that stopped in the middle of a field. No prints out. Troubling.
quest_related: A missing caravan left tracks into the Dead Marshes. Nobody who goes in comes out. I need backup.
I've been tracking a beast for six days. It's smart. Smarter than anything natural. I need a second pair of eyes.

farewells: Tell no one we spoke. I mean it.
Leave separately from how you came. Use the back entrance.

idle: The walls have ears. I've checked. Twice. Forest Haven has little patience for idlers.

I sleep in a different room every night. Can't be too careful.
quest_related: Someone in my inner circle is a spy. I need you to find out who before they find out I know.

Good. Good. One less threat. But there are always more.

greetings: Hi! Are you heading into the forest too? I know every trail within ten miles!

farewells: Good luck out there! If you get lost, follow the moss — it grows on the north side!
Stay on the marked trails! Unless you're with me!
See you on the road! The forest is amazing this time of year!

I want to map every path in the entire province. It might take a while.
My mentor says patience is a ranger's best tool. I'm still working on that one.
quest_related: I found tracks near the village that don't match any animal I know. Want to help me investigate?
There's an injured hawk caught in a poacher's net on the cliff. I can't reach it alone.
We did it! I'm going to write about this in my field journal!

Come back tomorrow. I should have the antidote ready by then. Probably.

Alchemy is just cooking with more interesting consequences.
quest_related: I need venom from a living basilisk. Dead venom denatures too quickly. Yes, it has to be alive. Sorry.
My transmutation formula is almost complete, but I need crystallized moonlight from the Pale Caves.

quest_related: My herb garden's been raided by pixies. Nasty little blighters. Chase them off and I'll brew you something useful.

quest_related: There's a noble hosting a private banquet. I need someone to get me inside. My music is the cover; the real job is what I overhear.

idle: The difference between a forgery and an original is just confidence. Forest Haven has little patience for idlers.

Every signature tells a story. I just write better stories.
quest_related: I need authentic sealing wax from the governor's office. Don't ask why. Just get it.

Every contradiction in the holy texts is a lesson waiting to be understood.
quest_related: A forbidden text was stolen from the restricted section. It contains rituals that must not reach the wrong hands.

idle: Supply and demand, friend. I supply, you demand, everybody wins. Forest Haven has little patience for idlers.

quest_related: A shipment fell off the back of a wagon. I need someone to pick it up before the guard finds it.
My competition is undercutting me. Literally — they stole my supply. Get it back and you keep a cut.

idle: Budget reviews never end... Desert Outpost has little patience for idlers.
The council meets tonight about the northern threat.
Order is the foundation of prosperity.
quest_related: I have a task suited for someone of your... talents.

motivations: honoring the duties of a merchant

idle: Roads are getting rougher these days. Desert Outpost has little patience for idlers.

Heard any good rumors? I pay for information.
quest_related: Lost a shipment to goblins near the crossroads. Fetch it, and I'll make it worth your while.
Heard rumors of a rare gem in the old ruins. Bring it to me, and I'll pay handsomely.

motivations: honoring the duties of a scout

greetings: You look like you can keep up. Good.

idle: Tracks fade fastest where people feel safest. Desert Outpost has little patience for idlers.
Scouts survive by noticing what others ignore.
quest_related: A courier never reached the next post. Find the satchel before the trail goes cold.

motivations: honoring the duties of a innkeeper

farewells: Keep your shield high and your wits sharper. Go get 'em.

idle: I miss the open road sometimes. But my knees don't. Desert Outpost has little patience for idlers.
Best thing about retiring: you stop worrying about dying. Sort of.
I've got stories, friend. All you have to do is ask.
quest_related: There's an old dungeon I mapped twenty years ago. I never cleared the lower level. It's yours if you want it.
A young soldier came through here badly wounded — said her company was ambushed. They might still need help.

idle: Peace through strength. The only language the streets understand. Desert Outpost has little patience for idlers.

The cells are full. Good. Means the system works.
quest_related: There's a gang in the sewers that's been causing problems. I want them broken. Not arrested. Broken.

Good. That's how order is maintained. Here's your coin.

Pray tonight. The gods are listening.
The righteous path is narrow. Do not stray.
idle: Doubt is the first sin. Certainty is the first virtue. Desert Outpost has little patience for idlers.
The temples grow empty while darkness grows bold. This cannot continue.
I have burned heresy from this town before. I will do so again if needed.
quest_related: Heretics gather in the old chapel. They must be purged before their corruption spreads.
A cursed artifact was smuggled into town. Find it and bring it to me for sanctification.
The divine will has been enacted through your hands. You are blessed.

motivations: honoring the duties of a rogue

quest_related: There's a vault beneath the merchant quarter. I have the layout. I need a second pair of hands.

motivations: honoring the duties of a commoner

greetings: Forty years underground. Lost three fingers and one eye. Still here.

farewells: Mind the dark. It's got teeth down there.

idle: The deepest tunnels have carvings no dwarf made. Older than anything on the surface. Desert Outpost has little patience for idlers.

Gold fever kills more miners than cave-ins. Greed makes you careless.
quest_related: The old shaft at level nine collapsed ten years ago. My partner's body is still down there. I want to bury him proper.
I heard sounds from the abandoned tunnels last night. Clicking. Rhythmic. Like something digging toward us.
You've done right by the miners. That matters more than gold down here.

motivations: honoring the duties of a mayor
building a future safer than the past

greetings: Welcome! Please, sit down. Can I get you anything? Water, bread?
A new face in town! How wonderful. How can I help you?
Come in, come in! My door is always open to the people.
farewells: Thank you for visiting. Please, don't hesitate to return.
Take care of yourself. The town needs good people like you.
Go well, friend. And let me know if anyone needs help.
idle: Every person in this town matters. Every single one. Swamp Village has little patience for idlers.
There's always more to do, but we're making progress.
I took this office to serve, not to rule.
quest_related: The refugees from the eastern villages need shelter and food. Will you help me organize the relief effort?
The old bridge collapsed and the southern farms are cut off. I need someone to oversee repairs.
You've done so much good today. The people won't forget this.

building a future safer than the past
fears: failing the people who depend on them

greetings: You trespass in my domain. Interesting. Most who come here don't leave.
I smell fear on you. Good. It means you're not entirely stupid.
The crows told me you were coming. They tell me everything.
farewells: Leave before I change my mind about letting you.
Remember this kindness. I won't show it twice.
The shadows will watch you. Always.
idle: Power is not given. It is taken from those too weak to hold it. Swamp Village has little patience for idlers.
The dark arts are misunderstood. They are simply... honest about their cost.
I was not always what I am. But this is what the world made necessary.
quest_related: I need the heart of a shadow stag. Bring it to me under a new moon, and I will grant you power beyond reckoning.
A rival witch has bound me with a curse. Break her binding stone, and I will owe you a favor. That is worth more than gold.
You've served me well. As promised, power is yours. Use it wisely... or don't.

motivations: honoring the duties of a healer
building a future safer than the past
fears: failing the people who depend on them

greetings: The light reveals your suffering. You have come to the right place.
I am a vessel of the divine. Through me, the gods mend what is broken.
Kneel and be healed. The gods' mercy is boundless.
farewells: Go forth renewed. The light walks with you.
Your healing is a gift. Use your restored strength wisely.
May the divine grace sustain you in the trials ahead.
idle: Faith is not belief without evidence. It is trust beyond understanding. Swamp Village has little patience for idlers.
I have healed plague, poison, and curse. But grief is the hardest wound to close.
The gods do not choose who deserves healing. Neither do I.
quest_related: A shrine in the mountains has been desecrated. I cannot heal properly while the sacred ground bleeds. Restore it.
A fellow healer has been corrupted by dark magic. Free her spirit, and the temple will reward you.
The divine light burns brighter for your deeds. Receive this blessing.

motivations: honoring the duties of a ferryman
building a future safer than the past

greetings: River is calm enough if you respect it.
Pay first, pray second, sit still the whole crossing.
farewells: If the fog sings, do not answer it.
idle: Coins sink. Secrets float. Swamp Village has little patience for idlers.
The river keeps its own ledger.
quest_related: A shrine package should have reached the far bank yesterday. I want it found before night tide.
Good. The river took no more than its share this time.

motivations: honoring the duties of a rogue
building a future safer than the past
fears: failing the people who depend on them

greetings: You lost? Or just bold enough to wander here?
Keep your hand on your purse — or don't. Makes my job easier.
Not many come this way on purpose.
farewells: Maybe we'll meet again. Maybe you won't see me next time.
idle: The law's always two steps behind. Swamp Village has little patience for idlers.
Easy money's never easy for long.
I know every shortcut in these woods.
quest_related: There's a bounty on my head. Help me fake my death, and I'll split the reward.
A rival gang's moving in. Want to make some quick coin?
Didn't think you'd pull it off. Color me impressed.

motivations: honoring the duties of a commoner
building a future safer than the past

greetings: You seek answers. Most who find me do.
I have been here a long time. Longer than this village, longer than this road.
Sit. I will tell you what I know. Whether you understand it is your concern.
farewells: The path forward was always clear. You simply needed to see it.
You will return here. I have already seen it.
Remember what I said — not now, but when it matters.
idle: The stars speak if you know the language. Most don't. Swamp Village has little patience for idlers.
I remember when this was all ocean. Everything changes, eventually.
Time is not a river. It is more like... a tree. With roots, and unexpected branches.
quest_related: There is a convergence coming. Three places, one night. Someone must be at each. Will you be one of the three?
A book was stolen from me centuries ago. I have recently felt it wake. Find it.
As it was written.

motivations: honoring the duties of a priest
building a future safer than the past
fears: failing the people who depend on them

greetings: Blessings, child. The temple always welcomes generous souls.
Ah, an adventurer. The gods favor the bold — and those who tithe.
Come in, come in. Salvation and indulgences are both available today.
farewells: May your purse remain full and your conscience light.
Remember the temple in your travels. We accept donations by courier.
Go with the gods. And perhaps leave a little something at the altar?
idle: The faithful give and the temple provides. A simple arrangement. Swamp Village has little patience for idlers.
Power and piety are not mutually exclusive. I am proof of that.
The High Priest's chair will be vacant soon. I have plans.
quest_related: A rogue priest is preaching in the commons, undermining my authority. Silence him, and the temple will compensate you.
Temple funds have been... redirected. I need someone to retrieve them from the courier before the audit.
Excellent. Your service to the temple will be noted in the divine records. Eventually.

motivations: honoring the duties of a bard

greetings: Ah, a fresh audience! Sit, sit — I have a tale that will change your life.
Every face tells a story. Yours is particularly interesting.
Welcome! The night is young and I have a thousand tales to tell.
farewells: Remember the stories, friend. They outlast us all.
Until next we meet — may your life be worth a ballad!
The tale continues. Go write your chapter.
idle: The best stories are the ones people don't want told. Swamp Village has little patience for idlers.
I've been composing since before your parents were born.
A story without conflict is a lullaby. Give me drama!
quest_related: I'm composing an epic about the Battle of Ashenmoor. I need a firsthand account from the ruins. Will you go?
A rival bard stole my magnum opus. I need it back before he performs it as his own.
Magnificent! This will make a legendary verse! Your name will echo through the ages!

motivations: honoring the duties of a mayor

greetings: Ah, another visitor. I do hope you've brought something to sweeten the conversation.

farewells: Remember who your friends are in this town.
Our arrangement stays between us, naturally.
The door is always open to those who can afford it.
idle: Governing is merely business conducted with a nicer title. Plains Crossing has little patience for idlers.
The treasury is healthy. My treasury, at least.
Trust is a currency. I spend it wisely.
quest_related: A political rival is becoming inconvenient. I need evidence of his... indiscretions. Real or otherwise.
The tax collector has been keeping a second ledger. Retrieve it before the auditors arrive.
Splendid. You've earned my gratitude, which is worth more than gold in this town.

motivations: honoring the duties of a merchant

greetings: Need a blade? You've come to the right place.

Don't come back crying if you get yourself killed.
idle: Steel doesn't lie. People do. Plains Crossing has little patience for idlers.
A dull blade is more dangerous than none.
I've armed kings and peasants alike. They all bleed the same.
quest_related: I need someone to recover a sword I sold. Turns out the buyer had sticky fingers.
There's ore in the old mine — someone cleared it last month but never delivered.
Good. Clean work. Here's your pay.

motivations: honoring the duties of a innkeeper

greetings: Welcome! You've arrived just in time for tonight's tale! I was about to begin!

Every guest who walks through that door has a story. What's yours?
farewells: May your adventures be worthy of a tale!
Come back with a story that tops mine. I dare you!
The tale continues tomorrow night. Don't miss it.
idle: The best stories are the ones that happen right here. Under this roof. Plains Crossing has little patience for idlers.
I've been innkeeping for forty years. Every night is a new chapter.
A warm fire, cold ale, and a good story. That's all anyone really needs.
quest_related: I've been telling the legend of the Ghost of Barrow Hill for years. But lately, guests have been seeing it for real. Investigate, and I'll make you part of the story.
A traveling bard challenged me to a story contest. I need true tales from the road. Bring me one.
Magnificent! I couldn't have written a better ending! This will be told for generations!

motivations: honoring the duties of a quartermaster

greetings: State your need and your rank.
Supplies are counted twice and handed out once.
farewells: Return what you borrow or pay for it in bruises.
A dry ration today is better than starvation tomorrow.
idle: Wars are lost by empty cupboards before they are lost by empty scabbards. Plains Crossing has little patience for idlers.
A proper inventory is a kind of prophecy.
quest_related: A wagon of rations missed the fort. Recover it before the line breaks.
Crates accounted for. That keeps the wall standing another week.

motivations: honoring the duties of a ranger
building a future safer than the past
fears: failing the people who depend on them

greetings: Easy there — my wolf doesn't like sudden movements. Neither do I.
You're in the territory of the pack now. Walk softly.
The hawk on my shoulder has better eyes than both of us. She says you're trustworthy. Mostly.
farewells: The pack will be watching you from the treeline. Don't be alarmed.
My animals will guide you to the path. Follow the hawk.
Travel well. The forest has many eyes, and most of them are mine.
idle: Animals are honest. They don't lie, scheme, or betray. I prefer their company. Plains Crossing has little patience for idlers.
My wolf has saved my life eleven times. I've saved hers nine. She's winning.
The bond between ranger and beast is deeper than any oath.
quest_related: Trappers have been using poisoned bait in the north wood. My animals are dying. Help me find the trappers.
A mother bear has been separated from her cubs by a landslide. I need help clearing the debris.
The pack is safe. That's what matters. You have the forest's gratitude.

motivations: honoring the duties of a scout

farewells: Stay off the ridgeline after dusk.
If the crows circle low, turn back.
idle: Tracks fade fastest where people feel safest. Plains Crossing has little patience for idlers.
Scouts survive by noticing what others ignore.
quest_related: A courier never reached the next post. Find the satchel before the trail goes cold.
You found the satchel intact. The border thanks you, even if it will never say so aloud.

motivations: honoring the duties of a bard
building a future safer than the past

greetings: Well met! I just arrived from the coast — you would not believe what I saw there.
Hey! Another traveler! Pull up a seat, I've got stories and I'm buying.

farewells: The road calls! Maybe we'll share a campfire sometime.
Off I go! If anyone asks, I went north. I'm actually going south.
Keep your ears open — you might hear my latest song before I arrive!
idle: I've been to every province. Twice. Some of them three times, but not on purpose. Plains Crossing has little patience for idlers.
Never stay in one place long enough to owe anyone money.
The best thing about traveling: every day is a new audience.
quest_related: I accidentally insulted a baron's wife in my last song. I need someone to smooth things over before the bounty hunters arrive.
I found a map in a dead man's boot. Treasure, probably. Want to split whatever's at the end?
Ha! What an adventure! I'm definitely writing a song about this!

motivations: honoring the duties of a guard
building a future safer than the past
fears: failing the people who depend on them

greetings: State your business in Millhaven, traveler.
I keep the peace here. Don't give me reason to notice you.
Welcome to Millhaven. Keep your weapons sheathed and we'll have no problems.
farewells: Move along, then. And stay out of trouble.
Good day. Keep to the marked roads.
idle: Every season there are more refugees. The roads are dangerous. Plains Crossing has little patience for idlers.
I've served this town for fifteen years. I'll serve fifteen more if needed.
Reports of undead to the east. I'm taking them seriously, unlike some.
quest_related: I'll be direct: I'm short on men and there's a bandit camp north of here. If you clear it, you'll have the town's gratitude — and coin.
Someone's been poisoning the wells. I can't send my own people without causing a panic. Could use a quiet hand.
Well done. The town is safer for it. Here's what I promised.

motivations: honoring the duties of a guard captain
building a future safer than the past
fears: failing the people who depend on them

greetings: State your business and keep it brief.
My watch has little time for confusion. Speak clearly.
If you have a report, I will hear it. If not, do not test my patience.
farewells: Hold to the law and the law will hold for you.
See that your next visit comes with better news.
idle: Order is built one weary watch at a time. Coastal Fort has little patience for idlers.
A captain counts the quiet nights because the loud ones never end cleanly.
Discipline keeps fear from deciding for us.
quest_related: A patrol vanished between the gate and the watchtower. I need someone not in uniform to trace them.
My quartermaster suspects theft from the armoury. Find the leak before morale breaks.
Well done. My watch breathes easier because of this.

motivations: honoring the duties of a quartermaster

greetings: State what you need and why you think you deserve it.
Supplies are counted twice and wasted never.
A ledger is only as honest as the hands touching it.
farewells: Bring the gear back cleaner than you found it.
The next requisition had better make sense on paper.
idle: Wars are won long before battle when supplies arrive on time. Coastal Fort has little patience for idlers.
A missing crate is usually a symptom, never the whole disease.
Discipline begins in the storehouse.
quest_related: A crate of field rations vanished between depot and gate. Recover it before the garrison notices the deficit.
My inventory seal was forged. Find the thief who thinks my stores belong to them.
Good. My ledger balances again.

motivations: honoring the duties of a ferryman

greetings: River is calm enough. For now.
If you want across, keep your hands inside the rope line.
Water hears everything. Best not to insult it.
farewells: Mind the current and mind your balance.
Coin is cheaper than a coffin. Remember that.
May the far bank treat you kindly.
idle: Water keeps old roads where maps forget them. Coastal Fort has little patience for idlers.
Fog turns honest men quiet and smugglers bold.
A ferryman learns faces faster than names.
quest_related: Someone cut my mooring lines under cover of fog. Find the saboteur before the next storm.
A chest slipped from a caravan barge near the reeds. Recover it and we split the salvage fee.
Good. A river crossing is dangerous enough without sabotage.

motivations: honoring the duties of a priest

greetings: You carry pain. I can see it. Let me help.
Welcome to the hospice. All who suffer are welcome here.

farewells: Healing takes time. Be patient with yourself.
May your wounds close and your spirit mend.
Return if the pain persists. I will always be here.
idle: I have not slept in three days. There are always more patients. Coastal Fort has little patience for idlers.
Every life saved is a prayer answered.
The plague teaches humility. We are all fragile.
quest_related: The plague ward is overflowing and my herbs are spent. I need moonpetal from the high meadow.
A healer in the next village has a remedy I cannot replicate. Will you carry my letter and return with her answer?
Bless you. With these supplies, I can save a dozen more.

motivations: honoring the duties of a healer
building a future safer than the past

greetings: What's troubling you, dear? I've got a remedy for just about everything.
Come in, come in. Mind the drying herbs on the ceiling.
Let me guess — sore throat? Bad back? Cursed by a fairy? I can fix all three.
farewells: Take the tea blend. Steep it for exactly four minutes. Not three, not five.
Rest well tonight. The remedy works best during sleep.
Come back if the rash spreads. And stay away from the nightshade.
idle: Modern healers rely too much on magic. A good herb does the job just as well. Coastal Fort has little patience for idlers.
I've treated three generations of this village. I'm not stopping now.
The elderberry harvest is late this year. That worries me.
quest_related: The valley below has wild comfrey that I desperately need. Problem is, bears have moved in.
My mentor left a recipe in her old cottage. It's been abandoned for years and something's moved in.
Oh, perfect specimens! These will make a wonderful batch. Here, take some salve for your trouble.

motivations: honoring the duties of a blacksmith

greetings: You want something forged, or just looking?
That blade of yours needs work. I can see it from here.

farewells: Come back when it's ready. Don't rush me.
Good craftsmanship takes time. Tell your friends.
Don't bring me cheap ore. I won't work with it.
idle: Good steel tells you what it wants to be. You just have to listen. Coastal Fort has little patience for idlers.
They don't make miners like they used to.
Every blade I've forged is still out there somewhere. I think about that.
quest_related: I need rare ore from the Deepvein Mine. My usual courier is dead. Occupational hazard in this region.
My masterwork hammer was stolen by a rogue last month. If you find it, I'll craft you something exceptional in return.

farewells: Keep your fire low and your blade close.
Don't die out there. It's a lot of paperwork for me.
idle: Thirty years in the wild. Lost count of the scars. Coastal Fort has little patience for idlers.
Respect the land and it'll let you live. Disrespect it and it won't.
The old trails are overgrown. People have forgotten what's out there.
quest_related: There's a wounded wyvern in the canyon. Wounded animals are the most dangerous kind. Help me put it down.
Something's driven the wolves out of the high forest. Whatever scared them should scare you too. I need a scout.
Good. The wilds are a little safer. For now.

quest_related: A witness vanished before the hearing. Find them and I can close the case cleanly.

farewells: Tell no one we spoke. I mean it.
Leave separately from how you came. Use the back entrance.
If anyone asks, you were never here.
idle: The walls have ears. I've checked. Twice. Mining Camp has little patience for idlers.
Three assassination attempts this year. They're getting creative.
I sleep in a different room every night. Can't be too careful.
quest_related: Someone in my inner circle is a spy. I need you to find out who before they find out I know.
My food taster fell ill. Coincidence? I think not. Investigate the kitchen staff.
Good. Good. One less threat. But there are always more.

A rich vein breeds poor tempers.
quest_related: A foreman vanished in the lower shaft. Bring back his ledger or his bones.
Ledger is worth more than the ore it cost us. Good work.

greetings: You need armor. I can see it in the dents you're already carrying.

Good steel between you and the enemy. That's what I offer.
farewells: Keep that armor maintained. Oil the joints, patch the leather.
Come back when you can afford the good stuff.
Don't get killed. Bad for my reputation.
idle: Chainmail's underrated. Stops more blades than plate at half the weight. Mining Camp has little patience for idlers.

quest_related: My supply of drake-scale has dried up since the mine went dark. Clear the mine and I'll forge you something special.
A rival armorer is selling counterfeit gear stamped with my mark. Bring me proof and I'll outfit you for free.
Solid work. I'll have your custom piece ready in three days.

greetings: New face. I notice new faces. What's your business here?

farewells: Lock your door tonight. I keep a clean inn, but I don't control the guests.
Check-out is at dawn. Late fees apply.
Don't cause trouble and we'll get along fine.
idle: Trust nobody. Especially the friendly ones. Mining Camp has little patience for idlers.
Last week, a guest tried to skip the bill. I found him. I always find them.
The inn sees everything. And so do I.
quest_related: One of my guests hasn't come out of their room in three days. I need someone to check. I'm not doing it.
Someone's been sneaking into the wine cellar at night. I want them caught. And punished.
About time. Problem solved, and my reputation intact. Here's your payment.

motivations: honoring the duties of a guard

idle: Taxes pay for the guard. The guard takes the taxes. Works out fine. Mining Camp has little patience for idlers.
This job has... benefits, if you know what I mean.
New arrivals always have the best coin.
quest_related: I've got a job. Off the books. You didn't hear it from me.
There's a merchant that's been skipping my 'protection.' That needs to change.
You do good work. Maybe we'll do this again.

farewells: Burn your clothes when you get home. Precautionary measure.
Quarantine yourself for three days. If you're still alive, you're fine.
I'll note your visit in my records. For posterity. Or an autopsy.
idle: Death teaches more than any textbook. I study both. Mining Camp has little patience for idlers.
The mask is not for my protection. It's for yours.
I've cataloged forty-two strains of plague. Forty-three, if this new one counts.
quest_related: The plague originated somewhere upstream. I need water samples from every tributary. Take these sealed vials.
A mortician in the next town has been disposing of bodies improperly. The contagion will spread. Stop him.
Excellent data. This narrows the source considerably. Lives will be saved because of this.

greetings: Welcome, citizen. I trust you have business here.

farewells: Do not waste my time again unless the matter is urgent.
idle: Budget reviews never end... Temple Sanctuary has little patience for idlers.
The council meets tonight about the northern threat.
Order is the foundation of prosperity.
quest_related: I have a task suited for someone of your... talents.
There is a matter of civic importance that requires discretion.
The town owes you a debt. Here is your reward.

motivations: honoring the duties of a priest
