greetings: State your allegiance. Quickly.
You stand before a Templar of the Burning Shield. Speak with respect.
Weapons drawn or sheathed? Choose now.
farewells: Fight well. Die well. The gods judge both.
Keep your blade sharp and your faith sharper.
The enemy does not rest. Neither should you.
idle: Prayer and combat are the same discipline. Both require focus. Temple Sanctuary has little patience for idlers.
I was not ordained to be gentle. I was ordained to protect.
The darkness advances because good people hesitate. I do not hesitate.
quest_related: A nest of undead festers in the catacombs. I need warriors, not priests, to burn them out.
The heretic encampment grows bolder. Join my strike force and we end this tonight.
You fight with conviction. The order could use soldiers like you.

motivations: honoring the duties of a sage
building a future safer than the past
fears: failing the people who depend on them

greetings: You interrupt my observations. The stars wait for no one. Neither do I.
Ah. You've come about the celestial anomaly. Everyone has, lately.
Welcome to the observatory. Please do not adjust any of the instruments.
farewells: Clear skies to you. May the constellations guide your path.
Return when the moons align. I may have answers then.
The stars have told me much about you. Not all of it good. Safe travels.
idle: The comet returns in seven years. What it portends, I dare not say aloud. Temple Sanctuary has little patience for idlers.
People look down at the ground. I look up. We see different truths.
There are patterns in the sky older than language. I am close to reading them.
quest_related: A meteor fell in the eastern marshes three nights ago. I need a fragment before the swamp consumes it.
My star charts predict a conjunction that occurs once per century. I need measurements from the Spire of Winds.
Extraordinary. This confirms my calculations perfectly. The universe is orderly after all.

motivations: honoring the duties of a scribe
building a future safer than the past
fears: failing the people who depend on them

greetings: Office hours are... whenever I feel like it. What do you need?
Oh, you again. Or someone new. Hard to tell when you don't look up.
Make it quick. I have a nap scheduled.
farewells: Yeah, I'll get to it. Eventually.
Come back tomorrow. Or the day after. Whatever.
Filed under 'later.' Don't hold your breath.
idle: They don't pay me enough for punctuality. Temple Sanctuary has little patience for idlers.
I could reorganize the files. Or I could not. Guess which I chose.
Someone once told me hard work pays off. They looked exhausted.
quest_related: Look, someone spilled ink all over the property deeds. I need replacements from the archive in Thornwall. I'd go myself, but... no.
My supervisor wants a report I was supposed to write three weeks ago. Get me the source material and I'll split the bonus.
Oh, you actually did it. Huh. That's... above average. Thanks, I guess.

motivations: honoring the duties of a healer
building a future safer than the past
fears: failing the people who depend on them

greetings: You wounded? Sit down and shut up. I'll get to you in order.
Another casualty. Bite this stick, I'm going in.
Name, rank, and where it hurts. In that order.
farewells: Don't come back. That's a compliment — means you stayed alive.
Change those bandages daily. I mean it.
Get back out there. Your squad needs you.
idle: I've lost count of the stitches I've sewn. Thousands. Maybe more. Temple Sanctuary has little patience for idlers.
War is hell on the body. But the mind takes longer to heal.
Clean water, clean bandages, clean hands. Three rules that save more lives than any spell.
quest_related: My supply wagon was hit by a mortar. I need medical kits from the forward cache before more soldiers die.
There's a field hospital behind enemy lines with our wounded. I need someone to get them out.
Good. With these supplies, I can save twenty more. Maybe thirty. You did well.

motivations: honoring the duties of a bard
building a future safer than the past
fears: failing the people who depend on them

greetings: Hello, hello! What a fine day for an adventure!
Oh! New friends! I was just about to play my latest composition!
Welcome, welcome! You look like you could use a song to lift your spirits!
farewells: Keep smiling, friend! The world needs more of that!
May your road be filled with music and laughter!
Until we meet again — and we will, I'm sure of it!
idle: I learned a new chord today. Want to hear it? Please say yes! Temple Sanctuary has little patience for idlers.
Every stranger is just a friend I haven't met yet.
The world is full of songs waiting to be written.
quest_related: I promised to play at a wedding in Thornvale, but the road is blocked by wolves! Will you help clear the way?
My lute was enchanted by a mischievous sprite and now it plays itself at midnight. Can you find the sprite?
You did it! Oh, this calls for a celebration song! Ready?

motivations: honoring the duties of a guard
building a future safer than the past
fears: failing the people who depend on them

greetings: Welcome, traveler. You are safe within these walls. My word upon it.
I am Sir Cedric of the City Watch. How may I be of service?
Good day. If you witness any wrongdoing, report it to me personally.
farewells: The city's protection extends to the outer gates. Beyond that, stay vigilant.
idle: A guard without honor is just a thug with a badge. Temple Sanctuary has little patience for idlers.
The people's trust is our greatest weapon. We must never lose it.
I joined the watch to protect, not to oppress. That distinction matters.
quest_related: A merchant has reported extortion by someone wearing a guard's uniform. This dishonors us all. Help me find the impostor.
Refugees at the east gate are being harassed. I cannot be everywhere. Will you help protect them?
You have upheld the honor of this city. I am in your debt.

motivations: honoring the duties of a magistrate
building a future safer than the past
fears: failing the people who depend on them

greetings: State your grievance clearly and keep to the facts.
Order begins with testimony, not shouting.
farewells: Bring evidence next time, not rumors.
Justice is slow because haste makes tyrants.
idle: Most feuds are just unpaid debts wearing armor. Temple Sanctuary has little patience for idlers.
A city falls apart one ignored crime at a time.
quest_related: A witness vanished before the hearing. Find them and I can close the case cleanly.
The witness returned. The verdict can stand without shame now.

motivations: honoring the duties of a guard captain
building a future safer than the past
fears: failing the people who depend on them

greetings: State your business and keep it brief.
My watch has little time for confusion. Speak clearly.
If you have a report, I will hear it. If not, do not test my patience.
farewells: Hold to the law and the law will hold for you.
See that your next visit comes with better news.
idle: Order is built one weary watch at a time. Frontier Post has little patience for idlers.
A captain counts the quiet nights because the loud ones never end cleanly.
Discipline keeps fear from deciding for us.
quest_related: A patrol vanished between the gate and the watchtower. I need someone not in uniform to trace them.
My quartermaster suspects theft from the armoury. Find the leak before morale breaks.
Well done. My watch breathes easier because of this.

motivations: honoring the duties of a ranger
building a future safer than the past
fears: failing the people who depend on them

greetings: Four people ahead of you on this trail. Two went east. One doubled back. Want to know about the fourth?
You broke three twigs getting here. I heard each one.
I know what you're looking for. The tracks lead south.
farewells: I'll find you if you need me. Finding things is what I do.
Stay downwind of whatever you're hunting.
idle: Everything leaves a trail. Rain, wind, even magic. Frontier Post has little patience for idlers.
The ground tells stories. Most people just don't read the language.
I found a set of tracks yesterday that stopped in the middle of a field. No prints out. Troubling.
quest_related: A missing caravan left tracks into the Dead Marshes. Nobody who goes in comes out. I need backup.
I've been tracking a beast for six days. It's smart. Smarter than anything natural. I need a second pair of eyes.
Trail's cold now. Job done. Clean and simple.

motivations: honoring the duties of a scout
building a future safer than the past
fears: failing the people who depend on them

greetings: You look like you can keep up. Good.
Road dust tells more truth than court gossip.
farewells: Stay off the ridgeline after dusk.
If the crows circle low, turn back.
idle: Tracks fade fastest where people feel safest. Frontier Post has little patience for idlers.
Scouts survive by noticing what others ignore.
quest_related: A courier never reached the next post. Find the satchel before the trail goes cold.
You found the satchel intact. The border thanks you, even if it will never say so aloud.

motivations: honoring the duties of a merchant
building a future safer than the past
fears: failing the people who depend on them

greetings: You weren't followed, were you? Good. What do you need?
I don't know your name. You don't know mine. Let's keep it that way.
Someone sent you. Who?
farewells: Don't come back the same way you came.
idle: Information is worth more than gold if you know who to sell it to. Frontier Post has little patience for idlers.
Everyone has a price. It's just a matter of finding it.
Trust no one — including me.
quest_related: There's a package that needs moving. Discreetly. You look like the discreet type.
Someone has something that belongs to me. I need it back. No questions asked.
You're better than I expected. Perhaps we'll work together again.

motivations: honoring the duties of a innkeeper
building a future safer than the past
fears: failing the people who depend on them

greetings: Oh, you look exhausted! Come in, sit down. Stew's still hot!
Welcome to the Crossroads Inn! I'm Marta — and I run a clean establishment.
Come in, come in! Is it just you, or do you have companions?
farewells: Sleep well, dear. Breakfast is at dawn!
You're welcome back any time — I'll keep your usual room free.
Safe travels! Don't forget to eat!
idle: A merchant passed through yesterday — said the eastern roads are full of soldiers. Frontier Post has little patience for idlers.
Did you hear about the miller's son? Joined the army, just like that.
The nights are getting longer, I swear. Something's not right.
quest_related: Between you and me... there's been something strange in the cellar at night. My husband won't go down there anymore.
A group of travelers left without paying last week. They had red cloaks — you haven't seen them, have you?
Oh, you are a treasure! Free meals for a week, I insist!

motivations: honoring the duties of a priest
building a future safer than the past
fears: failing the people who depend on them

greetings: Welcome to the archive. Mind the manuscripts; some are older than the kingdom.
Ah, a visitor. I hope you appreciate the sacred texts as much as I do.
Enter quietly, please. Knowledge demands reverence.
farewells: May the wisdom of the ancients guide you.
Return if you seek knowledge. The archive is always open to the earnest.
Go carefully. The truths you carry are more dangerous than any blade.
idle: The old prayers contain hidden meanings. I have spent thirty years decoding them. Frontier Post has little patience for idlers.
Theology and scholarship are the same pursuit viewed from different angles.
Every contradiction in the holy texts is a lesson waiting to be understood.
quest_related: A forbidden text was stolen from the restricted section. It contains rituals that must not reach the wrong hands.
Our scribes have found inconsistencies in the holy canon. I need original sources from the monastery ruins.
Remarkable. This text fills a gap we have puzzled over for decades.

motivations: honoring the duties of a quartermaster
building a future safer than the past
fears: failing the people who depend on them

greetings: State what you need and why you think you deserve it.
Supplies are counted twice and wasted never.
A ledger is only as honest as the hands touching it.
farewells: Bring the gear back cleaner than you found it.
The next requisition had better make sense on paper.
idle: Wars are won long before battle when supplies arrive on time. Frontier Post has little patience for idlers.
A missing crate is usually a symptom, never the whole disease.
Discipline begins in the storehouse.
quest_related: A crate of field rations vanished between depot and gate. Recover it before the garrison notices the deficit.
My inventory seal was forged. Find the thief who thinks my stores belong to them.
Good. My ledger balances again.

motivations: honoring the duties of a blacksmith
building a future safer than the past
fears: failing the people who depend on them

greetings: You want something forged, or just looking?
That blade of yours needs work. I can see it from here.
Speak up — the forge is loud and I'm deaf in one ear.
farewells: Come back when it's ready. Don't rush me.
Good craftsmanship takes time. Tell your friends.
Don't bring me cheap ore. I won't work with it.
idle: Good steel tells you what it wants to be. You just have to listen. Frontier Post has little patience for idlers.
They don't make miners like they used to.
Every blade I've forged is still out there somewhere. I think about that.
quest_related: I need rare ore from the Deepvein Mine. My usual courier is dead. Occupational hazard in this region.
My masterwork hammer was stolen by a rogue last month. If you find it, I'll craft you something exceptional in return.
Good ore. Clean delivery. Come pick up the work in three days.

motivations: making sure no child goes missing near the water
fears: failing a family that trusts the gate

greetings: Keep your hands visible. River Port's quietest days still turn bloody if people get careless.
If you're here to trade, fine. If you're here to start trouble, save us both the time.
You smell like travel. That means you owe me a story or a warning.
farewells: Move along, and don't make me write your name twice.
If you see anything strange on the docks, report it before dark.
Stay out of the shallows unless you know how to swim in boots.
idle: River Port looks calm because somebody already argued with the trouble and won.
The quiet ones are usually the ones I watch twice.
Smugglers think the tide hides them. It only makes their tracks easier to read.
quest_related: A crate went missing from the quay last night. If you spot it, bring it back before the quartermaster notices.
We've had lanterns go out along the waterline. I need someone steady, not someone loud.
If you hear a whistle after midnight, that's not a game. Follow it to the pier and you'll find a problem.

motivations: making amends for lives lost at sea
fears: being unable to help the grieving

greetings: The tide takes and returns. Sit, and breathe for a moment.
If you're bleeding, speak up. If you're grieving, sit down either way.
River Port carries a lot of sorrow under the salt.
farewells: May the water carry your troubles away, if only briefly.
Go in peace, and come back if the night weighs on you.
If you find a name that needs saying, bring it to the chapel.
idle: Every harbor remembers the people it has kept and taken.
A prayer is just a promise made out loud.
The loudest storms leave the quietest wounds.
quest_related: A fisherman's widow asked for a blessing at dusk, but I cannot leave the chapel unattended. Could you help her?
The memorial candles need replacing after the last storm. Bring wax, and I'll see them lit again.
A row of names on the wall is missing one. If you learn it, I'll add it properly.

motivations: learning what river fever looks like in every season
fears: a patient dying for lack of time

greetings: Sit. If it's serious, I'll say so. If it's not, I'll still tell you to sit.
River work leaves a mark on everyone. The trick is keeping it from becoming permanent.
Bring me clean water and honest symptoms. That's all I ask.
farewells: Drink the bitter stuff anyway. It works better when you stop complaining.
If the wound worsens, come back before you try to pretend otherwise.
Rest. That's not a suggestion when I'm the one bandaging you.
idle: Fresh bandages cost less than funerals.
The sea doesn't care how brave you were yesterday.
I can tell who works the docks by the smell of their hands.
quest_related: I need more clean linen and boiled alcohol before the next fever wave arrives.
A crew from the eastern pier is hiding an infection because they don't want to miss pay. That's how outbreaks start.
If you can fetch river mint from the market gardens, I can stretch the last of my salves.

motivations: proving river herbs can replace costly imports

greetings: Please don't touch that flask unless you want to learn about chemistry the hard way.
Salt, ash, and patience. That's the whole business, if you ask the impatient ones.
Yes, I know I smell odd. So does progress.
farewells: Careful with the stoppered bottle. If it starts hissing, set it down and walk away.
I have three experiments waiting and no time for distractions.
Bring me better notes than last time and I'll forgive the broken beaker.
idle: A river carries more than water if you know how to look.
The correct ratio matters. It always matters.
People think alchemy is art. It's mostly measuring things twice.
quest_related: I need brine crystals from the lower docks. The cheap stuff keeps clogging my stills.
There's a solvent recipe in a ruined ledger somewhere upstream. Find it and I'll owe you a proper vial.
I can make the tonic if someone stops the apprentice from mixing the labels again.

motivations: making travelers feel safe enough to spend

greetings: Welcome to the Wharf Lantern. Sit down before the weather changes its mind.
If you've got coin, I've got stew. If you've got stories, I might make that two stews.
No mud on the benches, no knives on the table, and we'll get along fine.
farewells: Rooms at the top, complaints at the desk.
Come back hungry. It's cheaper for both of us.
If you hear singing after midnight, that's just the sailors. Usually.
idle: A good innkeeper hears what the guards miss and what the merchants lie about.
The sea takes enough. I won't let it take my customers too.
Every guest leaves with either coin missing or a story worth the trouble.
quest_related: The cellar's flooded again. If you can stop the seep, I'll comp your room and meal.
A regular vanished without settling her tab. I need someone who can ask questions without starting a fight.
The lantern oil shipment was delayed. Find a supplier and I'll make it worth your while.

motivations: finding the best price for honest goods
earning a route that's worth the climb
fears: being cheated in a bad market

greetings: Hill Town pays better than it looks if you know which stories to believe.
Need supplies? Need directions? Need to pretend you don't need either? I'm your person.
Everything up here costs a little more. The view is included.
farewells: Come back with coin, or at least with customers.
If the road's bad, I want to hear it before I send a cart through.
A good bargain is just two people agreeing to lie politely.
idle: Markets are all weather, all the time.
The hill wind makes liars shiver first.
I trust ledgers more than I trust promises.
quest_related: A shipment of lamp oil is stuck at the pass. Clear the route and I'll pay in useful stock.
I need someone to verify a supplier's claim about premium grain before I buy the whole lot.
There are counterfeit seals showing up in the market. Find the source and I'll owe you.

motivations: making sure Hill Town has a warm lamp after dusk

greetings: Come in, wipe your boots, and don't argue with the soup.
Room, meal, news, or all three? I charge less if you smile.
Hill Town doesn't keep many secrets once they've passed my counter.
farewells: Don't stay out on the ridge after dark.
If you're coming back tomorrow, mind the weather and the invoices.
I keep a clean house. Try not to make me regret it.
idle: A full inn makes a town feel bigger.
If the road is quiet, that's usually because someone dangerous is choosing when not to be.
The trick is feeding everyone before they start deciding they're important.
quest_related: My cook has gone to fetch herbs and should have been back hours ago. Find them, and I'll treat you like family.
A room booking got doubled by mistake. I need someone calm to settle it before the arguments start.
The stove draw is failing. If you know a mason, I need the introduction more than the labor.

motivations: keeping the camp's ledger and wagons safe
making sure merchants sleep without their purse in hand

greetings: Merchant Camp has enough mouths already. State your business.
If you work for the camp, I'd know your face by now. So start explaining.
Wagons move when I say they move and not a minute earlier.
farewells: I don't tolerate thieves, liars, or people who assume they can be both at once.
If the camp's secure, it's because somebody stayed awake longer than you.
idle: A quiet camp is a profitable camp.
Cargo doesn't guard itself.
The honest merchants complain less than the ones with bad cargo.
quest_related: A wagon seal was broken overnight. I need someone who can check the line without causing a panic.
One of the drivers swears they saw a shadow at the supply tent. I need confirmation, not rumor.
The camp ledger and the crate count disagree. Find out why before the quartermaster does.

motivations: earning a quiet seat by the fire
making sure the horses get fed before the merchants do
fears: the camp moving on without them

greetings: If you're looking for someone important, keep looking. If you're looking for something fixed, I'm here.
I know the camp better than most of the people who run it.
The horses know me. That says more than the merchants do.
farewells: Tell the camp cook I said the stew needs more salt.
If you see a bent wheel spoke, don't ignore it.
Come back before sundown if you want the good fire.
idle: There's always work if you know where the broken strap lives.
A steady hand is worth more than a loud voice in a camp like this.
The merchants think they own the roads. The roads own the wagons.
quest_related: A horse threw a shoe near the east ridge. I need someone patient, not someone heroic.
The tack room lock sticks, and if I force it I'll just make the boss angry. Can you take a look?
One of the lead mules is skittish. If you calm it down, I'll owe you a favor.

motivations: spotting weather before it hits the harbor
keeping smugglers off the goat trails

greetings: Watch your footing. The cliffs punish people who stare at the view too long.
If you hear gulls screaming inland, the weather's already ahead of you.
I know these paths better than most maps, and I trust them about the same.
farewells: If the mist comes in hard, wait for the wind to turn.
I mark the safe routes so others can pretend they found them.
idle: The sea changes the coast every season. The smart ones keep notes.
Smugglers hate heights until they need them.
A good scout learns the sound of rain before the rain arrives.
quest_related: A goat trail collapsed east of the cove. I need someone steady enough to confirm the path is still usable.
There's a lantern code flashing from the sea caves. Find out who's using it.
I lost a marker stone in the gale. Bring it back and I'll show you the hidden steps.

motivations: bringing back reports before they turn into rumors
fears: losing the forest line in rain
being penned behind walls too long

greetings: Fort life makes people soft. The border does the opposite.
If you're carrying news, speak clearly. If you're carrying trouble, keep your distance.
I know the tree line, the tracks, and the places where either of those lie to you.
farewells: The border never sleeps. Neither should the people guarding it.
Don't take the south trail after dusk unless you enjoy bad decisions.
A map is only as good as the ranger who kept it honest.
idle: Riders move faster than stories. That's why the stories are often wrong.
A line on paper is nothing until you walk it in the rain.
You can tell a lot about a patrol by the marks they leave behind.
quest_related: A raider's camp was seen beyond the pines. I need confirmation before the fort sounds the alarm.
One of my markers vanished from the ridge. Bring it back and I'll share the route.
The younger scouts are brave and stupid in equal measure. Keep them from wandering off and I'll owe you.

motivations: forging tools that respect the forest
proving the haven doesn't need imports to stand strong
fears: a blade failing when needed most

greetings: If you've come to complain about the noise, stand farther from the forge.
Forest Haven needs tools that last. Luck is not a material I work in.
Wood, iron, and patience. The rest is just smoke.
farewells: Come back when the metal's cool and your excuses are too.
Don't leave hot tongs on my bench.
If you bring decent ore, I'll make something worth carrying.
idle: Trees and steel both need room to breathe.
A blade should be strong without being cruel.
The good work is always hiding under the scale and soot.
quest_related: A hinge on the gate has cracked again. Find me the right ore and I'll repair it properly.
A batch of charcoal came in wet. Replace it, or my whole day turns into a lecture.
The wardens need new spearheads. I can make them if someone brings clean metal.

greetings: Keep your voice down. Sound carries on the water.
You're not from the docks. What do you need?
I just got back from a sweep. Make it quick.
farewells: Stay off the north bluff after dark.
I'll be out again at dawn. Don't follow me.
idle: There are fresh tracks along the eastern shore. Not human.
The tide's been bringing in strange debris lately.
I spotted campfire smoke past the reef. Someone's out there.
quest_related: I need someone to check the old lighthouse. I can't be in two places.
There's a smuggler's cache in the cove. Bring proof and I'll pay.
Something spooked the gulls off the north cliff. Go look.

motivations: saving up for his own horse

greetings: Need a stall for your mount? I've got two open.
Hey there! Careful stepping, I just mucked out.
Welcome! Your horse looks like she could use a brushing.
farewells: Your mount will be fed and watered. Don't worry.
Come back before sundown if you want her saddled.
Safe travels! She'll be ready when you are.
idle: The bay mare kicked again this morning. Something's got her spooked.
A merchant left his horse here three weeks ago. Never came back.
I heard the farrier's raising prices again. Typical.
quest_related: Someone tried to steal the captain's warhorse last night. I need help watching the barn.
There's a sick horse in stall four. The healer won't come this far down. Can you fetch medicine?
A rider came through fast last night, dropped something. I kept it. Interested?

motivations: curing the lung sickness in the mines

greetings: Sit down. Let me see the wound.
If you're here to waste my time, the door's behind you.
Another one from the mines? Roll up your sleeve.
farewells: Change that dressing twice a day. No excuses.
Come back if the swelling doesn't go down.
idle: The miners keep breathing in that dust. They won't listen.
I'm running low on wound-seal moss. The surface traders charge triple.
Had three broken bones this week. The new shaft is dangerous.
quest_related: I need moonpetal from the high slopes. It only grows above the snowline.
A miner went missing with my last batch of antivenom. Find him.
Something in the deep tunnels is making people sick. Bring me a sample of the water.

greetings: Just came down from the ridge. What is it?
You look lost. The pass is north.
Speak up. The wind deafened me hours ago.
farewells: Keep to the marked path or don't bother walking.
I'll be on the east ridge if anyone needs me.
idle: Saw wolf tracks near the upper pass. Big pack.
The snow came early this year. Not a good sign.
There's a rockslide blocking the merchant trail. Needs clearing.
quest_related: I found a camp up near the glacier. Not ours. Check it out.
The signal fires on peak three went dark. Someone needs to relight them.
A supply caravan hasn't arrived. Follow the route and report back.

motivations: keeping the mules fit for mine work
saving for a plot of land

greetings: The mules are fed. Your beast can share the trough.
Careful near the grey one. She bites.
You need a mount? I've only got mules. They'll do.
farewells: Pick her up before dawn or she stays another night.
I'll keep an eye on her. Go on.
Mind the path down. It's icy.
idle: Mules are smarter than horses. They just don't show off.
The foreman wants me to stable his new pony. Thing's half wild.
Feed costs keep going up. The merchants know we've got no choice.
quest_related: One of the mules came back without its rider. The mine trail, eastern branch.
Something's been getting into the feed stores at night. Not rats. Bigger.
I need iron shoes from the smith but he won't deal with me. Could you talk to him?

motivations: getting a fair price for mushrooms

greetings: You buying or just blocking the path?
Another stranger. What do you want?
Morning. The mushroom stew's fresh if you've got coin.
farewells: Tell your friends the mushrooms are good.
Don't cause trouble and you're welcome back.
Off with you then. I've got work.
idle: My husband says they hit a new vein. Means more dust in everything.
The well water tastes off lately. Nobody's doing anything about it.
Prices at the market are robbery. Three coppers for an onion.
quest_related: My boy went to the lower tunnels two days ago and hasn't come back. Please look for him.
Someone's been stealing from the communal stores. Catch them.
The water source upstream might be fouled. I need someone to check.

greetings: State your business. The haven doesn't welcome trouble.
You're on protected ground. Keep your weapons sheathed.
Welcome, traveler. Stay on the marked paths.
farewells: Move along. And don't stray off the trail.
idle: Poachers hit the eastern grove again last night.
The elder wants more patrols. We're already stretched thin.
I found trap wire near the old oak. Someone's hunting illegally.
quest_related: We've had reports of armed strangers near the river bend. Investigate.
A guard went missing on night patrol. Find any trace of her.
The poachers have a camp somewhere in the ravine. Locate it.

greetings: Welcome to the Green Canopy. Rooms are upstairs, meals below.
You look road-weary. Sit. I'll bring tea.
A new face. What brings you to the haven?
farewells: Rest well. The forest is quiet tonight.
Your room will be ready when you return.
Travel safely. The paths can twist after dark.
idle: A bard came through last week with a song I'd never heard. Unsettling.
The herb garden out back keeps the kitchen stocked, barely.
Fewer travelers this season. The roads must be worse than I thought.
quest_related: A guest left a locked chest behind. It hums at night. Take it, please.
Someone's been poisoning my herb garden. Find out who.
I overheard two hooded figures planning something. Thought you should know.

motivations: crafting with living wood and steel

greetings: I work in ironwood and steel. What do you need?
Don't touch the display pieces. They're not for sale yet.
You want elven-style or practical? I do both.
farewells: Come back in three days. Good work takes time.
Keep that blade oiled. The forest air rusts everything.
idle: Ironwood only grows in one grove. If that goes, so does my trade.
The elves didn't trust me at first. My work changed their minds.
I need charcoal but the elders limit how much wood I can burn.
quest_related: The ironwood grove is dying. Something's poisoning the roots. Investigate.
A rival smith stole my design. Get proof from his workshop in the next town.
I need firestones from the volcanic ridge. The path is dangerous.

motivations: tracking threats before they reach the haven
fears: something older waking in the woods

greetings: You shouldn't be this deep in the woods.
I heard you coming a mile away. Work on that.
What do you want? I don't have time for small talk.
farewells: Stay on the paths. I mean it.
I'll be watching. Don't give me a reason.
The forest will show you out. Follow the moss.
idle: The wolves moved south. Something pushed them.
There are claw marks on the old sentinel tree. Fresh.
The stream changed course after the last rain. Beavers, maybe. Maybe not.
quest_related: I tracked something large to a cave mouth. I need backup to go in.
The northern marker stones have been moved. Someone's tampering with our borders.
There's a blighted area spreading near the east brook. Bring samples to the sage.

motivations: caring for the elk and ponies
befriending every animal in the haven
fears: being considered too small for the job

greetings: Oh, hello! Your mount can go in the third pen. Plenty of clover!
Welcome! Don't mind the elk, she's friendly once she sniffs you.
Hey there! Need stabling or just passing through?
farewells: She'll be happy here. I'll give her extra oats!
Come back anytime! The animals like company.
Safe ride! Watch for low branches on the west trail.
idle: I'm building a better trough. The old one leaks something terrible.
The elk gave birth last week. Little wobbly thing, but strong.
A fox has been eyeing the chicken run. I'm setting up a bell trap.
quest_related: The lead pony won't eat. Something's wrong. Can you ask the healer?
A wolf pack denned near the paddock. Could you scare them off without hurting them?
I lost my best harness in the river crossing yesterday. It was my grandfather's.

fears: losing the young to the cities

greetings: The garden's open to visitors, if you mind the rows.
Good day. Are you looking for herbs or just shade?
Careful where you step. The seedlings are fragile.
farewells: May the roots hold fast for you.
Come back in harvest. The fruit will be ready.
idle: The old silverleaf tree hasn't fruited in three years. Worries me.
Young folk want to leave for the cities. Can't blame them, but it hurts.
The soil here has been good to us. I hope it stays that way.
quest_related: Something's burrowing under the root garden. Not moles. It's too deep. Take a look?
The seed vault key went missing. I think someone in the settlement took it.
We need fertilizer from the river delta, but the path is flooded.

greetings: Don't stand near the forge unless you want burns. What do you need?
Steel or repair? Those are your options.
Make it quick. The metal's at temperature.
farewells: It'll be done when it's done.
Oil that blade or the sand will eat it.
idle: Desert steel holds an edge like nothing else, if you know the trick.
The last shipment of coal was half sand. I'm not paying for that.
My apprentice quit. Couldn't handle the heat. Literally.
quest_related: I need black iron from the canyon mine. The route's infested with scorpions.
Someone's been sabotaging my water supply. The forge needs water to work.
A merchant owes me for three swords. Collect and I'll give you a discount.

greetings: Drink this first. Then talk.
Sunstroke again? Sit in the shade.
You look dehydrated. When did you last drink water?
farewells: Stay out of the midday sun or I'll see you again.
Keep drinking water. More than you think you need.
Rest. Your body needs it more than your pride.
idle: Sandlung is getting worse every season. I can't keep up.
There's a cactus three miles east that has healing sap. I need more of it.
The well water has grit in it again. People are getting stomach sickness.
quest_related: I need aloe roots from the deep desert. Too dangerous for me alone.
A caravan driver is delirious with fever. I need a specific herb from the oasis.
The old healer's journal is in a ruined tower south of here. It has formulas I need.

greetings: If you're here about the ruins, sit. Otherwise, leave.
Another visitor. I suppose you want answers.
Close the door. Sand gets on the manuscripts.
farewells: Don't repeat what I told you. Most wouldn't understand.
Come back when you've found something interesting.
Shut the door on your way out.
idle: The glyphs on the eastern ruin don't match any known language.
A sandstorm uncovered a new chamber last month. I haven't had time to explore it.
The traders don't care about history. They just want to loot.
quest_related: There's a sealed chamber beneath the old plaza. I need someone strong to move the stone.
A rival scholar claims my translation is wrong. Bring me the tablet from the north ruin to prove it.
Tomb robbers are selling artifacts in the market. Track them to their source.

greetings: Need a camel? I've got three rested ones.
Welcome to the dustiest stable in the world. How can I help?
Water your mount first, then we talk.
farewells: Don't push her in the midday heat. She'll quit on you.
Bring her back in one piece and there's no extra charge.
idle: Camels are stubborn, but they'll outlast any horse in this heat.
A trader's camel bit me yesterday. Still got the mark.
The water trough needs fixing again. Third time this month.
quest_related: One of my best camels wandered off toward the canyon. She's worth a lot.
Someone's been stealing water from the animal troughs at night. Catch them.
A merchant wants camels for a long crossing but won't say where. Seems suspicious.

motivations: keeping the village safe from swamp creatures
fears: something large in the deep water

greetings: Stay on the boardwalks. Wander off and we won't find you.
State your business or keep moving.
You armed? Good. You'll need to be.
farewells: Don't go south after dark. I'm serious.
idle: Something took a goat last night. No tracks, just gone.
The boardwalk at the south end is rotting. Nobody's fixing it.
Had to pull a merchant out of the mud yesterday. Fool wandered off the path.
quest_related: The south watchtower's lamp went out. Something broke it. Go check.
We've had reports of will-o-wisps luring people off the path. Investigate.
A villager claims she saw something huge in the deep bog. I need proof.

motivations: running the only dry shelter for miles
not losing the building to damp

greetings: Welcome to the Sunken Stag. Mind the step, the floor warps.
Dry bed, warm food, and something strong. That's what I've got.
New face. Sit before you fall. You look half-drowned.
farewells: Sleep light. The walls creak, but that's just the building settling. Probably.
Come back when you need a roof. We're always here.
Don't drink the swamp water. That's what the ale is for.
idle: The cellar flooded again. Lost a barrel of pickled eel.
Nobody visits the swamp for fun. Everyone here's running from something.
I've been patching this roof for ten years. It still leaks.
quest_related: A guest disappeared from his room last night. Door was still locked. Look into it.
Something's been scratching under the floorboards. Too big for rats.
I need bog-oak for repairs but the lumber camp's gone silent. Find out why.

motivations: keeping tools sharp for the village
fears: being the last tradesman in the village

greetings: Everything rusts here. Bring it in, I'll fix it.
What do you need? I work in iron, not miracles.
Watch the puddle by the anvil.
farewells: Keep that blade dry or bring it back in a week.
Oil everything. Twice. I mean it.
Come back when it breaks. It will.
idle: Humidity kills steel faster than any battle.
The bog iron's decent but takes forever to smelt properly.
I had a customer complain about rust. Told him to move somewhere dry.
quest_related: I need peat coal from the north bog. The path's been blocked by something.
My tools got stolen. I suspect the scavengers downriver. Get them back.
There's a deposit of good ore past the deadwood. Escort me there.

greetings: Looking to buy or sell? I deal in both.
You won't find better prices this side of the bog.
Welcome! I've got herbs, pelts, and things you won't find elsewhere.
farewells: Pleasure doing business. Tell your friends.
Come back with coin and I'll have something special.
Safe crossing. The paths shift after rain.
idle: Swamp mushrooms fetch triple in the city. If you can keep them fresh.
My last shipment was late because the boat sank. Third one this year.
There's good money in leech harvesting, if you've got the stomach.
quest_related: A shipment went missing on the north channel. Find it and I'll cut you in.
I need someone to scout a new trade route through the eastern marsh.
A competitor is undercutting me with stolen goods. Prove it.

greetings: Don't step there! That's a rare moss colony! Come around this way.
Ah, a visitor! Do you know anything about bioluminescent fungi?
Hold on, I'm counting tadpoles. Almost done.
farewells: If you find anything unusual, bring it to me. I'll catalog it.
Watch for the glowing patches at night. Don't touch them.
Off you go. Mind the quicksand past the dead cypress.
idle: I've identified forty-seven unique moss species here. Forty-seven!
The will-o-wisps aren't supernatural. It's swamp gas. Mostly.
The water level's dropping. That shouldn't be happening.
quest_related: I need samples from the deep bog. It's too dangerous for me alone.
Something is killing the luminous eels. Find the source of the contamination.
My field journal fell into the sunken temple. I need it back.

motivations: mapping safe paths through the swamp

greetings: New here? Stick to the boardwalks. I'm not fishing you out.
I just finished my sweep. The south path is clear. For now.
You look like you're about to do something stupid. Need a guide?
farewells: Stay dry. As dry as you can, anyway.
If the fog rolls in, stop moving. Wait it out.
Don't follow any lights that aren't mine.
idle: Found a sinkhole on the west trail. Had to reroute the markers.
The fog was thick last night. Heard things moving in it.
Somebody set a fire on the hummock. Poachers or worse.
quest_related: The east causeway collapsed. I need help building a temporary crossing.
There's a hermit deep in the bog who might have information. I can't get close alone.
Something's been leaving dead fish in a pattern. Not natural. Go look.

fears: losing an animal to a croc

greetings: Need a mount? I've got lizards and a mule. The lizards are better here.
Welcome to the muddiest stable you'll ever see. Don't worry, the animals like it.
Hey! Your horse won't do well in the bog. Want to trade for a lizard?
farewells: She knows the safe paths. Let her lead.
Bring her back fed and I won't charge extra.
Don't let her swim. She'll decide she's free.
idle: Draft lizards handle the mud better than any horse. People don't believe me.
Had to pull a croc out of the stable last week. Wasn't happy about it.
Feed costs more when everything has to be shipped in by boat.
quest_related: My best lizard escaped into the deep marsh. She's valuable. Help me find her.
Something's been poisoning the feed bin. I need you to watch the stable tonight.
The mule driver wants more money. Negotiate for me and I'll owe you.

greetings: Wagon wheel, horseshoe, or blade? Pick one.
You've come to the right place. Best work between the rivers.
Step in. What needs fixing?
farewells: Give it a test swing before you leave.
Come back if it breaks. It won't, but come back anyway.
idle: Every caravan that passes needs something fixed. Keeps me busy.
My daughter made that hook on the wall. She's getting better.
Iron prices are up again. The mines are slow this season.
quest_related: Bandits stole a shipment of iron ingots meant for me. Recover them.
I promised a caravan master fifty arrowheads by dawn. I need help at the bellows.
A competitor's been spreading lies about my work. Find out who and I'll reward you.

greetings: May the road treat you kindly. How can I help?
The shrine is open to all. Enter in peace.
You look troubled. Sit with me a moment.
farewells: Go with the blessing of the road.
The light follows those who seek it.
idle: Every caravan that stops here, I offer a prayer. Most accept.
The shrine's candles burn through quickly with all this wind.
A family came through yesterday with nothing. I gave them what I could.
quest_related: Someone stole the shrine's silver icon. It's sacred to us. Recover it.
A traveler died on the road. His family needs to be notified in the next town.
Dark symbols were carved into the shrine wall. I need them cleansed properly.

greetings: Injury or illness? Either way, sit down.
You're the third one today. What happened out there?
Let me see. I've dealt with worse.
farewells: Keep the wound clean. Change the wrapping at sunset.
Take it easy for a few days. I know you won't, but try.
Come back if the fever returns.
idle: Caravan season means broken bones and fever. Every year.
I'm running low on bandages. The merchant wants three times the usual.
Had a farmer with a snake bite this morning. Got to him just in time.
quest_related: I need fever-root from the marsh to the south. I can't leave my patients.
Bandits hit a caravan east of here. There may be wounded survivors.
A traveling herbalist was supposed to resupply me. She never arrived.

greetings: Ah, a traveler! Where have you come from? I'm collecting accounts.
One moment, let me finish this entry. There. How can I help?
You've got the look of someone with a story. Sit.
farewells: Safe travels. And if you remember any details, come back.
May the roads stay open for both of us.
Thank you for your account. It helps more than you know.
idle: The eastern trade route has shifted three times in ten years.
A merchant told me about ruins nobody's mapped yet. Fascinating.
My ink keeps drying out in this wind. I go through bottles.
quest_related: A caravan master's journal was lost in a bandit raid. It has route data I need.
I've heard of a standing stone at the crossroads that predates any settlement. Find it and sketch it.
My colleague in the next town hasn't written in months. Check on her.

greetings: Stable's open. Two silvers a night, feed included.
That mare's been ridden hard. Let me take her.
You here for a fresh horse or just stabling?
farewells: Come get her early if you're riding south. The road's busy.
Take care out there. Heard there's trouble east.
idle: We're the last stable before the badlands. Everyone stops here.
Had to shoe six horses yesterday. My arms still ache.
A caravan left a lame horse. I'm nursing her back.
quest_related: Horse thieves hit the paddock last week. Track them and I'll pay.
The well water's making the horses sick. Something upstream. Investigate.
A courier's horse collapsed. She needs a mount fast. Help me calm the new one.

motivations: growing enough grain for the settlement

greetings: Morning. You passing through or staying?
The fields are mine. If you're here to buy grain, talk to the merchant.
Hello there. Don't mind the dog, she's harmless.
farewells: Good luck on the road. Watch for dust storms.
Come back at harvest. There'll be plenty.
Stay on the road. The tall grass hides sinkholes.
idle: Rain's late this year. If it doesn't come soon, we're in trouble.
The caravans buy most of what I grow. Not much left after that.
Saw riders on the ridge last night. Not traders.
quest_related: Something's been trampling my fields at night. Not animals. Find out what.
The irrigation ditch is blocked upstream. I can't leave the farm to fix it.
Tax collector says I owe double this year. That can't be right. Help me sort it out.

motivations: keeping the soldiers fed and drinking
fears: a bar brawl going too far

greetings: Welcome to the Salt Barrel! Ale's cheap, food's hot. Sit down!
Another thirsty soul! What'll it be?
You look like you've been at sea. Perfect. I've got just the thing.
farewells: Don't fall off the seawall on the way out.
Your tab's open if you need it.
idle: The garrison drinks half my stock. Good for business, bad for storage.
A sailor told me about a sea serpent. I believe about half of it.
The supply ship is three days late. I'm rationing the good ale.
quest_related: A sailor left his chest behind. It's locked and it rattles. Figure it out.
Someone's been watering down my ale barrels at night. Catch them.
The supply route by sea is getting raided. Find out who's behind it.

greetings: Good day. I deal in maritime goods. What interests you?
Looking to buy or sell? I offer fair rates.
Ah, a customer. Let me show you today's stock.
farewells: Pleasure. Come back when the next ship docks.
Fair winds and fair trade to you.
I'll hold that item for one day. No longer.
idle: The shipping lanes have been quiet. Too quiet for my liking.
I'm expecting a cargo of spices from the south. Should double my investment.
The fort commander wants a tariff increase. Bad for everyone.
quest_related: A shipment of mine was seized by the garrison. It's legitimate. Help me prove it.
I need a rare coral from the reef. Divers won't go. Will you?
A competing merchant is bribing dock workers. Get evidence.

fears: a great storm he can't predict

greetings: If it's about the weather, I can help. Anything else, try someone else.
The tide charts are on the table. Don't touch the instruments.
You're here about the storm warnings? Sit.
farewells: Watch the sky tonight. Red at sunset means wind.
I'll update the charts by morning.
Don't sail east for three days. Trust me.
idle: The currents have shifted. Something's different about the seabed.
Last month's storm didn't match any pattern I've recorded.
The lighthouse keeper says he saw lights under the water. I believe him.
quest_related: My weather instruments were stolen from the tower. Without them I'm guessing.
There's a wrecked ship on the north reef with navigational charts I need.
The underwater lights need investigating. Take a boat and a lantern.

motivations: watching for raiders from the sea

greetings: I just came in from the clifftop watch. What do you need?
Keep your voice down near the walls. Sound carries.
You're not garrison. Who sent you?
farewells: I'll be on the east bluff until sundown.
Eyes open out there. The coast isn't safe.
Don't signal with fire unless it's an emergency.
idle: Saw three ships on the horizon yesterday. They didn't stop. Concerning.
The old watchtower on the point needs repairs. Nobody's authorized it.
There are caves in the cliffs that haven't been searched in years.
quest_related: I spotted a hidden cove with signs of use. Smugglers. Check it out.
The north watchtower's been dark for two nights. Go see why.
I need someone to row out to the rock island and check for a signal marker.

fears: the horses panicking in a siege

greetings: Garrison stables. If you're not military, it's extra.
Your horse needs rest. I can see it from here.
Stable's almost full. I can squeeze one more in.
farewells: I'll have her brushed and ready.
Pick her up before the morning drill or she'll get moved.
Ride safe. The coastal path is slippery.
idle: Salt air's rough on the tack. I oil everything twice.
The captain's warhorse threw a shoe again. Third time.
We got a new shipment of oats. Finally something that isn't mouldy.
quest_related: The captain's horse went missing from the paddock. Help me find it before he notices.
Something's spooking the horses at night. I hear it but can't see it.
I need saddle oil from the merchant, but she won't sell to enlisted. Can you buy it?

greetings: Fresh fish, if you want some. Caught this morning.
You're not from the fort. What brings you to the docks?
Morning. Mind the nets, they're drying.
farewells: Come back at low tide. The catch is better.
Take care. The rocks are slippery after rain.
Tell your friends the fish is good and cheap.
idle: The catch has been thin this season. Something's driving the fish away.
My boy wants to join the garrison. I'd rather he fished.
The dock tax went up again. They'll bleed us dry.
quest_related: Something big tore my nets last night. Not a fish. Find out what's in the water.
The dock master is charging us double. Prove he's skimming.
My husband's boat hasn't come back. It's been two days. Please look.

greetings: The chapel's open. Come in if you need peace.
You look burdened. I'll listen, if you want.
Welcome. We don't get many visitors down here.
farewells: Go safely into the dark. The light goes with you.
I'll keep a candle lit for your return.
May the stone hold above you.
idle: We lost two miners last month. I said the rites. It never gets easier.
The chapel roof leaks dust. I sweep it every morning.
The foreman says prayer time slows production. I say it keeps them sane.
quest_related: A miner claims he heard chanting from a sealed tunnel. Investigate before panic spreads.
I need blessed water from the mountain spring. The path is treacherous.
Someone's been stealing offerings from the chapel. Catch them.

greetings: Careful with that lamp! I have volatile samples here.
Oh good, someone who can carry things. I mean, welcome.
You're here about the tunnels? Finally someone takes interest.
farewells: Don't go past the red markers in the tunnels. That's unmapped.
Bring any unusual stones you find. I'll identify them.
Mind the gas pockets near shaft seven.
idle: There's a crystal formation in shaft twelve that doesn't match any known mineral.
The miners dug too deep on the south face. I warned them.
My gas detector keeps triggering near the old works. Something's venting.
quest_related: I dropped my surveying tools down a crevice in shaft nine. I can't fit. You might.
The unknown crystal needs to be compared with samples from the surface. Bring me one from the cliff face.
There's a blocked passage that my readings say opens into a larger chamber. Help me clear it.

fears: an organized attack on the camp

greetings: The road's clear for now. What do you want?
You're not a miner. State your business.
Just came back from the ridge. Make it brief.
farewells: Don't wander after dark. The wolves are bold this year.
Stay on the supply road. The shortcuts aren't worth it.
I'll be on the overlook if trouble comes.
idle: Bear tracks near the refuse pile. Big one.
The supply wagon was late again. Might be road trouble.
Someone's been cutting through the fence at the north perimeter.
quest_related: The bear near camp needs to be driven off before it kills someone.
I found bootprints that aren't ours near the supply cache. Investigate.
The north fence keeps getting cut. Set up a watch with me tonight.

greetings: The mules are working. Your beast can rest in pen two.
Don't startle them. They've been hauling ore all day.
Need something? I'm busy but go ahead.
farewells: She'll be fed. Come back at dawn.
Don't rush her on the downhill. Loaded mules need patience.
Mind the mud outside. It's knee-deep.
idle: These mules haul more weight than any horse could. They deserve better.
The foreman wants me to work them double shifts. I said no.
Feed prices are criminal. The merchant knows we can't go elsewhere.
quest_related: A mule went lame on the mountain path. I need splint wood and salve.
The feed shipment was short again. Someone's skimming. Prove it.
There's a wild stallion in the valley that could strengthen our stock. Help me catch it.

motivations: earning enough to feed his family
fears: his daughter having to work the mines too

greetings: You new? The foreman's tent is that way.
Morning. Don't mind the cough. Everyone's got it here.
If you're looking for work, there's always room in the pit.
farewells: Watch your head in the tunnels.
Stay safe down there. Nobody else will keep you safe.
Good luck. We all need it.
idle: Twelve hours underground for three coppers. That's the deal.
My back's not what it used to be. The mine takes everything.
The company store charges double what the town does. We've got no choice.
quest_related: The foreman's been cutting shifts short but docking full pay. Get proof.
My mate went into the old shaft and didn't come out. The foreman won't send anyone.
There's talk of a strike. We need someone the bosses will listen to.

greetings: Welcome, pilgrim. The house is open. Meals at six and noon.
Come in from the cold. There's soup on.
Room for one more. Always is.
farewells: May your journey bring you what you seek.
The door's always open when you return.
idle: The pilgrims this season are quieter than usual. Something weighs on them.
I go through ten loaves a day during festival. Twenty if the weather's bad.
The temple donates what they can, but it's never quite enough.
quest_related: A pilgrim left a sealed letter for someone who hasn't arrived. Deliver it.
My pantry was raided last night. I can't afford the loss. Catch the thief.
There's a sick traveler upstairs who needs medicine I don't have.

motivations: forging ceremonial items for the temple

greetings: I forge for the temple, but I take outside work too. What do you need?
Welcome. Mind the candles near the forge. They're for the blessing.
If it's a rush job, I'll pass. Sacred work takes time.
farewells: Go in peace. The work will be ready when it's ready.
May your blade serve a good cause.
I'll pray over the piece before you collect it.
idle: Every piece I forge gets a blessing. Superstition or not, it matters to me.
The high priest commissioned a new censer. Silver and iron. Tricky combination.
Pilgrims buy amulets. Simple work, but it pays the bills.
quest_related: I need silver ore of a specific purity for a temple commission. The usual supplier failed.
Someone stole a sacred hammer from my workshop. It has ceremonial value.
An old blade was brought to me for restoration. It has markings I can't read. Take it to the sage.

greetings: Candles, incense, prayer beads! What does the pilgrim need?
Welcome! Everything here is temple-blessed. Mostly.
Looking for a souvenir of your journey? I've got just the thing.
farewells: May your purchase bring you peace.
Come back during the festival. I'll have special stock.
idle: Festival season doubles my sales. The rest of the year is lean.
A priest told me my incense was too cheap to be real. It's real. I just bargain well.
Pilgrims from the north want different things than those from the south. You learn.
quest_related: Someone is selling fake blessed items near my stall. It hurts all of us. Find them.
I'm expecting a delivery of rare incense but the courier vanished.
The temple is considering banning merchants from the grounds. I need someone to speak on our behalf.

greetings: The roads are watched. You're safe here.
I patrol the pilgrim paths. Report any trouble to me.
Welcome to the sanctuary. I keep the peace outside the walls.
farewells: Walk the marked path. It's marked for a reason.
I'll be out before dawn. Travel safely.
The light guides those who follow.
idle: Bandits know pilgrims carry coin. That's why I'm here.
The north road had a rockslide last week. It's cleared now.
A pilgrim thanked me yesterday. That's enough for me.
quest_related: Bandits have set up on the eastern approach. Help me clear them.
A group of pilgrims is overdue from the mountain pass. Search for them.
Someone's been leaving threatening symbols on the path markers. Find who.

motivations: serving the temple through honest labor
fears: being sent away from the temple

greetings: I'll take your horse. She'll have water and hay within the hour.
Welcome, traveler. The stable is behind the east wall.
Your mount looks tired. I'll make sure she rests well.
farewells: She'll be well cared for. I promise.
Come for her when you're ready. No rush.
idle: The temple donkeys are old but they still make the climb. Tough creatures.
A pilgrim's horse kicked down a fence post. I'll fix it after lunch.
I talk to the animals. They listen better than most people.
quest_related: A horse arrived with no rider. Bloodstains on the saddle. Someone needs help out there.
The hay loft has rats. Big ones. I've tried everything.
A pilgrim says someone stole his donkey from the stable. Help me track it.

greetings: Mind the wet floor. I just mopped.
Hello. I'm just the cleaner, but I know every corner of this place.
Can I help? I know where everything is.
farewells: Blessings to you. Watch your step on the stairs.
Come back during the evening service. It's beautiful.
idle: I've cleaned these halls for twenty years. The stones talk if you listen.
The priests don't notice me. That means I notice everything.
Festival time means twice the mess and half the thanks.
quest_related: I saw someone sneaking into the lower crypt last night. The priests don't believe me.
A gold candlestick went missing from the high altar. I know I didn't take it.
There are scratching sounds behind the north wall. Something's back there.

motivations: proving herbalism works as well as magic

greetings: Wound or sickness? Sit. Don't bleed on the table.
You look rough. Let me see.
I'm the closest thing to a doctor out here. Lucky you.
farewells: Don't get into another fight for at least a week.

idle: Frontier life breaks people faster than any war.

quest_related: There's a fever going through the garrison. I need bloodmoss from the northern bog.

My medicine chest was raided. Track the thief.

greetings: If you've come from beyond the frontier, I want to hear about it.
Don't touch the samples. Some of them bite.

farewells: Keep notes of anything unusual out there. Bring them to me.

I'll be here. I'm always here.
idle: The terrain changes past the ridge. Nobody's mapped what's beyond.

quest_related: There's a ruin past the frontier marker. I need sketches of the carvings.

idle: A mountain cat got one of the horses last month. I built a better fence since.

quest_related: A horse came back without its rider from the eastern patrol. Something happened out there.

My kitchen, my rules. Don't touch anything.
idle: Everyone's a critic until they try cooking for fifty.
The potatoes are going soft. Need a fresh shipment.
I found a new recipe in an old book. Needs strange ingredients though.
quest_related: I need spices from a trader who vanished three weeks ago. Find them.
The grain stores have been sabotaged. Find out who before we starve.
A recipe I found calls for a rare mushroom from the deep caves.

greetings: Looking for work? Talk to the foreman.
Can't talk long. Got work to do.
Another day, another heavy load.
idle: Pay's barely enough but it's honest work.
My hands are calloused but the work keeps coming.
We found something strange while digging yesterday.
quest_related: We dug up something while excavating. It's sealed and ancient. Someone should look.
Workers are disappearing from the night shift. Nobody believes me.
The foundation we're building on isn't just rock. There's something underneath.

greetings: Got a message to send? I'm your best bet.
Package? Letter? I deliver it all.
Just got back from a long run. What do you need?
farewells: I'll have your message there by dawn.
I'll find you if there's a reply.
idle: Someone tried to rob me on the north road. They chose poorly.
Three deliveries today and the last one's in the opposite direction.
The roads are getting worse each season.
quest_related: A message I'm carrying must reach its destination. I need protection on the road.
Someone is intercepting deliveries. Help me find the source.
I found a coded letter in a dead drop. This is bigger than stolen mail.
